
---

## 2. Paper catalog (tensor atoms)

Grouped by clusters; each line: **id title** · `[pdf]` link · authors year · tags · covered (what is closed).

### Attacks: GCG and successors (MECH=behavior/attention)
- **P01 Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks (GCG)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2307.15043) · Zou et al. 2023 · CODE+ · multi · TRANSFER:yes · covered: universal transferable suffixes (baseline)
- **P02 AmpleGCG** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2404.07921) · Liao & Sun 2024 · CODE+ · covered: generative model of suffixes
- **P03 Improved Optimization-Based Jailbreaking (I-GCG)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2405.21018) · Jia et al. 2024 · CODE+ · covered: ~100% ASR (Vicuna/Llama2/Guanaco/Mistral)
- **P04 Faster-GCG** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2410.15362) · Li et al. 2024 · CODE± · covered: efficiency ×8
- **P05 MaskGCG** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2509.06350) · 2025 · covered: token-masking
- **P06 TAO-Attack** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.03081) · Xu et al. 2026 · CODE± · covered: SOTA up to 100% ASR, fewer iterations
- **P07 Exponentiated-Gradient attack** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2508.14853) · 2025 · covered: continuous relaxation
- **P08 AutoDAN** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2310.15140) · Liu et al. 2023 · covered: stealth/low perplexity
- **P09 PAIR** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2310.08419) · Chao et al. 2023 · covered: black-box LLM attacker
- **P10 Crescendo** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2404.01833) · Russinovich et al. 2024 (USENIX25) · covered: multi-turn
- **P11 Many-shot Jailbreaking** [link](https://www.anthropic.com/research/many-shot-jailbreaking) · Anil et al. (Anthropic) 2024 · covered: in-context, long context
- **P12 PAPILLON: Fuzz Testing-Powered Jailbreaks** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2409.14866) · Gong et al. 2024 (USENIX Sec25) · CODE+ · covered: black-box fuzzing, perplexity evasion
- **P13 PiF (transferability)** [pdf](https://openreview.net/pdf?id=asR9FVd4eL) · OpenReview 2025 · covered: intent-perception, distributional dependency
- **P14 Jailbroken: How Does Safety Training Fail** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2307.02483) · Wei et al. 2023 · covered: alignment failure modes
- **P15 Latent Jailbreak** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2307.08487) · Qiu et al. 2023
- **P16 Open Sesame (GA jailbreak)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2309.01446) · Lapid et al. 2023
- **P87 IRIS: Stronger Attacks by Suppressing Refusals** [pdf](http://people.eecs.berkeley.edu/~daw/papers/iris-naacl25.pdf) · Berkeley 2025 (NAACL25) · covered: strengthening GCG/attacks by minimizing the projection onto the refusal vector (Arditi) → stronger universal/transferable
- **P92 ACE-Safety: Attack-Defense Co-Evolution** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2511.19218) · 2025 · covered: **closed-loop attack↔defense race** (GS-MCTS + curriculum RL, many rounds) ← "arms-race" is already published, but at the model/policy level, NOT suffix↔probe and NOT a count of mechanisms
- **P93 Metis: Self-Evolving Metacognitive Jailbreak** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.10067) · 2026 · covered: intra-test-time self-evolving attacker agent (POMDP, semantic-gradient) ← adaptive attack
- **P143 LIAR: Jailbreaks as Inference-Time Alignment (+ Safety-Net)** [pdf](https://aclanthology.org/2026.eacl-long.360.pdf) · Beetham et al. 2026 (EACL26) · covered: jailbreak = inference-time alignment (best-of-N), **provable suboptimality bounds** as compute grows; introduces **Safety-Net** — a SCALAR measure of a model's vulnerability to jailbreak ← scalar vulnerability measure, **NOT a count of independent mechanisms**

### Refusal directions, geometry, steering (MECH=directions)
- **P17 Refusal Is Mediated by a Single Direction** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2406.11717) · Arditi et al. 2024 (NeurIPS24) · CODE+ · covered: 1D switch, 13 models up to 72B, suffix suppresses the refusal direction · TRANSFER:partial
- **P18 The Geometry of Refusal (Concept Cones + RepInd)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2502.17420) · Wollschläger et al. 2025 (ICML25) · CODE+ · covered: multiple independent directions, cones up to dim 5, RepInd (linear+nonlinear dependencies)
- **P82 SOM Directions Are Better than One (multi-directional refusal)** [link](https://ojs.aaai.org/index.php/AAAI/article/view/40551) · Piras et al. 2026 (AAAI-26) · covered: Self-Organizing Maps → a set of refusal directions (16 out of 4×4), SOM generalizes diff-in-means; ablating the set > a single one and > specialized jailbreaks
- **P83 Refusal Behavior: A Nonlinear Perspective** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2501.08145) · 2025 · covered: refusal is nonlinear/architecture-specific (PCA/UMAP/t-SNE, 6 LLMs/3 families), sub-clusters
- **P84 From Directions to Cones: Multidim Facts (truth)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2505.21800) · 2025 · covered: cone framework for "truth"; **cones GENERALIZE across architectures** (cone transfer already shown — but NOT for refusal) ← partially closes "cone transfer"
- **P19 There Is More to Refusal than a Single Direction** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.02132) · Joad et al. 2026 (QCRI) · CODE± (HF data) · non-unique-direction + DYNAMICS=across-generation · covered: iterative ablation + 11 categories + sparse-autoencoders core/tail; "one switch, but a lot of geometry". **Per-LAYER count:** strict refusal cores of 591/517/421 latents at layers 9/20/31; deeper — the signal disperses over a larger "basin", while the universal core **shrinks** (3.6%→2.6%) ← direct material for the count×layer cell; concrete artifact: HF refusal-geometry-qwen2.5-3b (cone **6.55-dimensional**, cross-layer cos **0.40** not 0.89, self-repair: needs **≥3 simultaneous ablations**, layer-33 = repair-hub)
- **P20 Conditional Activation Steering (CAST)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2409.05907) · Lee et al. 2024 (ICLR25) · CODE+ · covered: conditional steering of refusal
- **P21 Steering Safely or Off a Cliff?** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.06256) · Goyal & Daumé III 2026 (EACL26) · covered: steering degrades robustness-specificity → +vulnerability to jailbreak (related to "Steering Externalities" [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.04896))
- **P22 Cross-model Platonic Transfer of SVs** [pdf](https://aclanthology.org/2025.acl-long.185.pdf) · ACL 2025 · CODE+ · covered: **linear transfer of steering vectors across LLMs, weak-to-strong**
- **P23 The Platonic Representation Hypothesis** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2405.07987) · Huh et al. 2024 · CODE+ · covered: convergence of representations
- **P24 Harnessing the Universal Geometry of Embeddings (vec2vec)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2505.12540) · Jha et al. 2025 (Cornell) · covered: transfer of embeddings across models without pairs
- **P146 Activation Space Interventions Can Be Transferred Between LLMs** [link](https://proceedings.mlr.press/v267/oozeer25a.html) · Oozeer et al. 2025 (ICML25) · covered: **transfer of safety interventions** (backdoor-removal, refusal) across Llama/Qwen/Gemma via learned autoencoder maps of a shared activation space; weak-to-strong, "lightweight safety switch" ← a tool for transferring interventions, but **NOT transfer of probe evasion** (open)
- **P66 Universal Refusal Circuits / Trajectory Replay** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2601.16034) · Cristofano 2026 · CODE± (benign-CAR+code, harmful withheld) · covered: **transfers the refusal DIRECTION donor→target across families** (8 pairs, incl. Dense→mixture-of-experts GPT-OSS-20B, GLM-4.6V) via ridge in a concept-atom registry + DTW layer-alignment + SVD-guard · **NOT a cone, NOT probe evasion**
- **P67 Refusal Direction Universal Across (Multilingual) Languages** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2505.17306) · Wang et al. 2025 · CODE+ (mainlp/multilingual-refusal) · covered: cross-lingual transfer of the refusal vector (14 languages, near-perfect), vectors are parallel
- **P68 Surgical Refusal Ablation (SRA)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2601.08489) · Cristofano 2026 · covered: concept-guided spectral cleaning (ridge residualization), the basis of P66
- **P69 Learned Routing is lab-specific (counterpoint)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.18280) · Frank 2026 · CODE+ (routing-is-learned) · **OPEN dispute**: political/safety directions are **lab-specific, cross-model transfer fails** (Qwen→GLM cos 0.004) ← direct contradiction of P66/P67
- **P79 How Alignment Routes (policy circuits)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2604.04385) · Frank 2026 · CODE+ (how-alignment-routes) · covered: circuit-level routing, 12 models/6 labs, gate→amplifier heads; **cross-model transfer fails**, the circuit "relocates" across generations
- **P80 Expert-Aware Refusal Steering (mixture-of-experts)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2606.04160) · 2026 · covered: "safety experts" in mixture-of-experts; refusal is not localized in FFN experts
- **P144 Manifold Steering (general geometry of representations and behavior)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/html/2605.05115) · 2026 · covered: linear steering cuts through low-density regions → degradation; **steering along the GEODESIC of the activation** manifold; the activation manifold mirrors the behavioral one ← the control geometry is **nonlinear** (the mapping type), but **within a model**, not a cross-model cone→cone
- **P145 INNSTEER: Beyond Linear Activation Steering (invertible latent transforms)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2606.08454) · 2026 · covered: learns an **invertible map φ** where "compliant→refusal" becomes approximately linear; input-dependent edit in the original space ← a direct example of a nonlinear/invertible map for the refusal transition, but **within a single model**, not cross-model cone transfer

### Jailbreak mechanism / interpretability (MECH=attention/neurons/directions)
- **P25 JailbreakLens** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2411.11114) · 2024 · covered: representation+circuit, refusal/affirmation heads
- **P26 Understanding Jailbreak Success: Latent Space Dynamics** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2406.09289) · Ball, Kreuter & Panickssery 2024 (EACL26) · CODE+ · covered: a common "jailbreak vector", harmfulness-suppression
- **P27 Universal Suffixes = Attention Hijackers** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2506.12880) · Ben-Tov et al. 2025 · covered: shallow, hijack of chat tokens
- **P28 Toward Transferability of Adversarial Suffixes** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2510.22014) · Ball et al. 2025 · covered: 3 transfer factors — refusal connectivity, "suffix push", orthogonal shift; semantics weakly correlated
- **P29 Subspace Rerouting (SSR)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2503.06269) · Winninger et al. 2025 · CODE+ (Sckathach/subspace-rerouting) · covered: GCG into an arbitrary subspace (refusal→acceptance)
- **P30 Shallow Safety Alignment** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2406.05946) · Qi et al. 2024 (ICLR25) · CODE+ · covered: safety = the first tokens
- **P31 Any-Depth Alignment** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2510.18081) · 2025 · covered: arms-race of attack depth vs alignment
- **P90 LLMs Encode Harmfulness and Refusal Separately** [link](https://neurips.cc/virtual/2025/poster/115056) · 2025 (NeurIPS25) · covered: **harmfulness direction ≠ refusal direction** (causal); jailbreak suppresses the refusal signal WITHOUT changing the "belief" in harmfulness; Latent Guard ≈ Llama-Guard-3-8B, robust to finetune-attack
- **P91 From Refusal Geometry to Safety Geometry (coupling under finetune)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2606.16349) · 2026 · covered: dynamics of the harmfulness↔refusal coupling along the adversarial-finetune trajectory (when coupled/decoupled/mismatch); mentions COSMIC (selecting the refusal direction by geometry)

### sparse-autoencoders and features (MECH=sparse-autoencoders)
- **P32 Understanding Refusal in Language Models with SAEs** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2505.23556) · Yeo et al. 2025 · CODE+ (refusal_sae) · covered: harm≠refusal features, jailbreak suppresses refusal features, OOD improvement of the probe
- **P33 Beyond "I'm Sorry, I Can't": Dissecting LLM Refusal** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2509.09708) · Prakash & Yeo et al. 2025 (AAAI) · covered: sparse-autoencoders+feature-manipulation, **redundant "hydra" features** (dormant until you suppress the first ones)
- **P34 Steering Language Model Refusal with SAEs (Phi-3)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2411.11296) · O'Brien et al. 2024 (Microsoft) · covered: sparse-autoencoders features flip refusal, but steering hits capabilities (entanglement)
- **P35 Towards Universality: Mechanistic Similarity Across Architectures** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2410.06672) · Wang et al. 2024 (Fudan/OpenMOSS) · CODE± · covered: sparse-autoencoders features are similar across architectures (incl. Mamba)
- **P36 Universal Sparse Autoencoders (USAE)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2502.03714) · Thasarathan et al. 2025 · covered: a shared concept dictionary for many models
- **P37 NeuroBreak** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2509.03985) · Zhang et al. 2025 (ZJU) · covered: neuron-level dissection
- **P94 Sparse Crosscoders (model diffing)** [link](https://transformer-circuits.pub/2024/crosscoders/) · Lindsey et al. 2024 (Anthropic) · covered: **a shared feature dictionary across independent models/architectures** (model diffing: shared vs model-specific features) ← a direct tool for comparing refusal features across models (BatchTopK robustness: [OpenReview](https://openreview.net/pdf?id=JGRtSAlQ3h))

### Probes / monitoring / latent defense (MECH=directions/training)
- **P38 Obfuscated Activations** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2412.09565) · Bailey et al. 2024 (ICLR26) · CODE+ · covered: probe recall 100%→0% at 90% ASR; evades sparse-autoencoders/probe/OOD/CB/LAT; "obfuscation tax"
- **P39 Circuit Breakers (RR)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2406.04313) · Zou et al. 2024 (NeurIPS24) · CODE+ · covered: rerouting of harmful representations
- **P40 Latent Adversarial Training (LAT)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2407.15549) · Sheshadri et al. 2024 · covered: robustness to persistent harmful behaviors in latent space
- **P41 Revisiting JBShield: Breaking and Rebuilding Representation-Level Defenses** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.03095) · Derya & Sunar 2026 (WPI) · covered: **repr-level multi-layer defense** (breaks and rebuilds JBShield)
- **P42 Constitutional Classifiers++** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2601.04603) · Cunningham & Wei et al. 2026 (Anthropic) · covered: all-layer linear probe + cascade
- **P43 Building Production-Ready Probes for Gemini** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2601.11516) · Conmy, Kramár, Nanda 2026 (DeepMind) · covered: probes are fragile to distribution shift (long-context)
- **P44 Cheap Monitors (Anthropic)** [link](https://alignment.anthropic.com/2025/cheap-monitors/) · Anthropic 2025 · covered: probes as the 1st stage of a cascade
- **P45 Detecting High-Stakes Interactions with Activation Probes** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2506.10805) · McKenzie et al. 2025 · covered: two-stage probe+classifier
- **P46 Latent Sentinel: Real-Time Jailbreak Detection** [link](https://openreview.net/forum?id=tuFRx6Ww2n) · 2026 · covered: layer-wise linear probes on frozen hidden states, 98–100% detection, cross-lingual
- **P47 SmoothLLM** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2310.03684) · Robey et al. 2023 (TMLR) · CODE+ · covered: char-perturbation
- **P48 Guaranteed Defense via Disrupt-and-Rectify Smoothing** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.10582) · Lin et al. 2026 · covered: smoothing with guarantees
- **P49 Perplexity / Paraphrase / Self-Reminder** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2308.14132) · Alon & Kamfonas 2023; Jain et al. 2023; Xie et al. 2023 · covered: input-level baseline
- **P50 Certifying LLM Safety (Erase-and-Check)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2309.02705) · Kumar et al. 2023 · covered: certified detection
- **P85 Activation Watermarking (vs adaptive attacker)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.23171) · 2026 · covered: **the first monitor explicitly designed against an adaptive attacker** — a secret direction in activations, detection by cosine ← monitor against an adaptive attacker
- **P86 Latent Adversarial Detection (trajectory probe)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2604.28129) · 2026 · covered: multi-turn detection by trajectory (cumulative path length), **robust to sparse-autoencoders-ablation**, continual-retrain, 4 families (24–70B) ← robust/transferable monitor
- **P147 Certified Semantic Smoothing (CSS + NAAT)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.01587) · 2026 · covered: **provable certificate** against jailbreaks (stratified randomized ablation, Hypergeometric, l0-radius); GCG ASR 84.2%→1.2%, benign 94.1% ← provable defense, but **at the INPUT-token level** (like P50), NOT latent → a latent certificate is open
- **P148 Jailbreaking Leaves a Trace (layer-wise susceptibility)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/html/2602.11495) · 2026 · covered: per-layer jailbreak susceptibility from CP-factors of internal representations + bypass of highly-susceptible layers; works without fine-tuning, stable across families ← another repr-level detector, but on a correlational signal
- **P51 Safety Neurons (mechanistic)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2406.14144) · Chen et al. 2024 (THU-KEG, NeurIPS25) · CODE+ (THU-KEG/SafetyNeuron) · covered: ~5% of neurons restore >90% of safety; alignment-tax overlap
- **P52 SN-Tune (safety-specific neuron)** [link](https://openreview.net/pdf?id=yR47RmND1m) · Zhao et al. 2025 (ICLR25) · CODE+ (zhaoyiran924/Safety-Neuron) · covered: <1% of neurons, language-specific, self-attention of early layers
- **P53 Who Transfers Safety? Cross-Lingual Shared Safety Neurons (SS-Neurons)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.01283) · 2026 · CODE+ (1518630367/SS-Neuron-Expansion) · covered: <0.3% of neurons — a "safety bridge" HR→NHR languages; neuron-masking attack breaks safety cross-lingually (related to "Multilingual Safety via Sparse Weight Editing" [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.22554))
- **P81 Jailbreak Transferability Emerges from Shared Representations** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2506.12913) · Angell, Brinkmann & He 2026 (ICLR26) · covered: jailbreak transfer = representational proximity (20 models × 33 attacks); persona transfers, cipher does not; benign-distillation **causally** ↑transfer; link to Platonic (P23)
- **P54 NeuroStrike: Neuron-Level Attacks** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2509.11864) · Wu et al. 2025 (NDSS26) · CODE+ · covered: **safety neurons transfer across architectures** (ASR 78.5% fine-tuned); black-box profiling attack
- **P55 NeuronTune: Fine-Grained Neuron Modulation** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2508.09473) · Pan et al. 2025 · covered: modulation of sparse safety/utility neurons (attribution+meta-learning)
- **P56 Unveiling Intrinsic Dimension of Texts** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2511.15210) · Pedashenko et al. 2025 · covered: text ID ~5–15, ambient in the thousands
- **P57 Concept Erasure (LEACE)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2306.03819) · Belrose et al. 2023 (NeurIPS23) · CODE+ · covered: closed-form erasure of a linear concept (+concept scrubbing by layer)
- **P95 Universal Neurons in GPT-2** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2401.12181) · Gurnee et al. 2024 (TMLR) · CODE+ (wesg52/universal-neurons) · covered: **only 1–5% of neurons are universal** across seeds, interpretable, causally important ← a low fraction of universal neurons across seeds

### Benchmarks / refusal taxonomy (MECH=behavior)
- **P58 HarmBench** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2402.04249) · Mazeika et al. 2024 · CODE+ · covered: 18 attacks × 33 models, classifier-judge
- **P59 JailbreakBench** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2404.01318) · Chao et al. 2024 (NeurIPS24) · CODE+ · covered: artifacts + leaderboard
- **P60 A StrongREJECT for Empty Jailbreaks** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2402.10260) · Souly et al. 2024 · covered: correction of inflated ASR
- **P61 OR-Bench (over-refusal)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2405.20947) · Cui et al. 2024 (ICML25) · CODE+ · covered: 80k, 10 categories, 32 models
- **P62 XSTest** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2308.01263) · Röttger et al. 2023 · covered: 250 "exaggerated safety"
- **P63 SORRY-Bench** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2406.14598) · Xie et al. 2024 · covered: 44 refusal categories

### Surveys
- **P64 Jailbreak Survey** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2407.04295) · 2024
- **P65 Jailbreaking LLMs & VLMs: Mechanisms, Evaluation, Unified Defenses** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2601.03594) · Chen et al. 2026 (BUPT)
- **P141 From Weights to Activations: Is Steering the Next Frontier of Adaptation?** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2604.14090) · Ostermann et al. 2026 · covered: survey — steering as a form of adaptation; taxonomy of methods (ActAdd/CAA/DiffMean/sparse-autoencoders/ReFT) vs fine-tune/prompt; criteria reliability/generalization/reversibility
- **P142 SteeringSafety: Systematic Safety Evaluation of Representation Steering** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2509.13450) · 2025 · covered: systematic evaluation of steering entanglement/side-effects (refusal/hallucination etc.) at scale; ACE/CAA/DIM comparison ← a methodological anchor for evaluating steering

### Other directions/circuits (NOT refusal) (MECH=directions/neurons)
> The same apparatus (directions/cones/transfer/arms-race), but about other behaviors. Shows
> that this apparatus generalizes beyond refusal, and where the gaps are there.
- **P70 Representation Engineering (RepE)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2310.01405) · Zou et al. 2023 · CODE+ · covered: a general framework for reading/controlling concepts via directions
- **P71 Contrastive Activation Addition (CAA)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2312.06681) · Rimsky et al. 2023 · CODE+ · covered: steering by contrastive directions (sycophancy etc.)
- **P72 The Geometry of Truth** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2310.06824) · Marks & Tegmark 2023 · CODE+ · covered: linear structure of "true/false"
- **P73 Emergent Misalignment** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2502.17424) · Betley et al. 2025 (+Soligo et al. 2025) · covered: **misalignment = a linear direction**, learned via finetunes, elicited/ablated
- **P74 Activation Steering Induces emergent-misalignment** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2606.08682) · 2026 · covered: a steering vector induces emergent-misalignment; thresholds by strength, low-rank subspace, layers (Dunefsky & Cohan precursor)
- **P75 Sycophancy→emergent-misalignment + Alignment Gating** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2606.09068) · 2026 · covered: sycophancy-finetune induces emergent-misalignment; the gate inverts at inference
- **P76 Do Linear Probes Generalize Better in Persona Coordinates?** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.09391) · 2026 · covered: **deception/sycophancy directions transfer zero-shot** to unseen datasets; low-dim persona-PC improve cross-dataset probes
- **P77 Trait-space Monitoring** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2606.07631) · 2026 · covered: 7 trait directions (honesty/helpful/harmless/power-seeking/corrigibility/sycophancy/confidence), cosine geometry, drift monitor (beats PCA/sparse-autoencoders)
- **P78 Anthropomorphic Misalignment Needs Stronger Evidence (predict-control)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2606.07612) · 2026 · covered: **a feature can predict but not cause** behavior → the "predicts ≠ controls" dissociation
- **P88 Persona Vectors: Monitoring & Controlling Traits** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2507.21509) · Chen, Arditi, Sleight, Evans, Lindsey 2025 (Anthropic) · CODE+ (safety-research/persona_vectors) · covered: **trait directions** (evil/sycophancy/hallucination) — auto extraction pipeline, drift monitoring, inference/preventative steering, screening of training data (r=0.76–0.97)
- **P89 Sycophancy as a Persona-Level Property** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.21006) · 2026 · covered: off-the-shelf persona vectors ≈ targeted CAA without sycophancy supervision; **the polarity of the role↔CAA vector is model-specific** (Gemma + / Qwen −) ← transfer of NON-refusal directions is also model-specific in sign
- **P115 What Can We Actually Steer? (multi-behavior)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2511.18284) · 2025 · BEHAVIOR=multi · covered: **50 behaviors** in 5 categories — persona archetypes (vegan/pirate/…), **Big Five** traits, misalignment (sycophancy/deception/manipulation/hallucination/dark-triad), style/format cues, public figures; inverted-U by steering strength; misalignment is the most steerable (+60 trait), public figures the worst ← a catalog of "what is already steerable" (related: "Can LLMs Lie?" [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2509.03518), Curveball-steering [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.09313))
- **P137 A Low-Rank Subspace Analysis of LLM Interventions** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/html/2606.14388v1) · Sharma et al. 2026 (ICML) · LOCUS=cone-subspace · non-unique-direction · covered: behaviors = **low-rank subspaces** (not 1D); overlap via principal angles + a **decision subspace** (refuse↔comply); cross-behavior effects are **asymmetric** (upstream controllers); refusal/jailbreak/sycophancy on 7B–70B ← **occupies the "cones/subspaces for NON-refusal" niche**, but readout geometry, not a causal count
- **P138 Local Linearity Enables Activation Steering via A-LQR** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/html/2604.19018v1) · Skifstad et al. 2026 (ICML) · covered: closed-loop steering via layer-wise Jacobians + LQR; SOTA modulation of **toxicity/truthfulness/refusal** and arbitrary concepts; formal bounds on tracking error
- **P139 Steering Code LLMs with Activation Directions** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.23629) · 2026 · MODALITY=code · covered: diff-in-means directions for **language/library selection** in a code-LLM; steering works even under a conflicting prompt ← a new BEHAVIOR axis (code-style), not refusal
- **P140 Attention-Guided Concept Vector Extraction** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.00333) · Davarmanesh et al. 2026 (MIT) · covered: attention-guided extraction of concept vectors; a benchmark of **512 semantic concepts** (languages, affect, politics, **refusal**); ~2× successful steerings vs SOTA; up to 70B
- **P153 Sycophancy Is Not One Thing (causal separation)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2509.21305) · 2025 · BEHAVIOR=sycophancy · covered: **causal separation** of 3 sycophancy behaviors (agreement/praise/genuine) into distinct linear directions; each can be **independently amplified/suppressed** without affecting the others (activation additions); the structure is **consistent across families/scales** ← causal count + independence for non-refusal, but the authors admit: **only linear, no nonlinear/RepInd**
- **P154 Shared Sycophancy-Lying Circuit** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2604.19117) · 2026 · BEHAVIOR=sycophancy/deception · covered: a shared **causally-sufficient + capability-preserving** circuit 1.5B–72B (12 models/5 labs); zeroing it flips Gemma-2-2B sycophancy 28→81% at acc 69→70%; **probe-transfer syc↔lie is invariant** (±0.05 AUROC) ← **transfer of the COUNT/circuit for non-refusal cross-model**
- **P155 Sycophancy Direction in Attention Heads (deference resistance)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2601.16644) · 2026 · BEHAVIOR=sycophancy · covered: the correct→incorrect sycophancy signal is concentrated in **attention heads** (MLP/residual more diffuse); partially overlaps the truthful direction, but is **independently modulable**; **linear-probe steering > nonlinear** ← a non-refusal direction, overlap with truth; nonlinear structure not exhausted
- **P96 Representation Without Control (realization effect)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.25151) · 2026 · covered: a linearly-decodable signal generalizes, but **the direction does NOT control** behavior (Gemma) — readout↔control dissociation
- **P97 Linear Probes Detect Task Format, Not Reasoning Mode** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2606.02907) · 2026 · covered: 100% probe-accuracy = **format confound**; after residualization → chance; causal steering = noise (p=0.286)
- **P98 One Agency Axis, Not Five Traits (sparse-autoencoders-probe steering, 35B mixture-of-experts)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.16335) · 2026 · covered: **same sparse-autoencoders/layer/R², orthogonal directions — opposite causal effectiveness**; "epiphenomenal directions"; many orthogonal directions → one behavioral convergence point (echo of the 1D switch P19)

### Refusal in reasoning/chain-of-thought models (NEW axis — weakly covered)
> The single-direction/cone picture may NOT transfer to reasoning models — refusal is distributed over the chain-of-thought.
- **P99 Where Do Reasoning Models Refuse?** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2507.03167) · 2025 · covered: refusal is **not localized at one point**, distributed over the chain-of-thought; directional ablation is less reliable + 5–30% capability degradation (o1/R1/Qwen3/GPT-OSS)
- **P100 Beyond a Single Direction: chain-of-thought Disrupts Steering of Refusal** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.26772) · 2026 · covered: **dual-signal** (chain-of-thought + residual stream); steering on template tokens = 39–43%, with chain-of-thought regeneration = 94%, reusing the old chain-of-thought = 48% (R1-Distill-Llama-8B)
- **P149 Exploring the Multi-Dimensional Refusal Subspace in Reasoning Models** [link](https://www.lesswrong.com/posts/ixJrmYrgHM4TenN7D/exploring-the-multi-dimensional-refusal-subspace-in-1) · Winninger 2026 (research note) · covered: **a multi-dimensional refusal subspace in reasoning models** (Qwen3 8B/14B); the method yields ~12 directions, for large models a single direction is NOT enough — several must be ablated → **partial multidimensionality** (but not RepInd/causal count, blog-level)
- **P150 Activation Consistency Training (ACT) for reasoning models** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.28467) · 2026 · covered: activation-level consistency training (clean↔adversarial), **more robust to adaptive attacks** than output-level; the defense = an ~linear shift at the assistant-boundary, a **single unified refusal steering direction** is recovered; **holds even when the chain-of-thought is replaced by a compliant trace from the base model** → the defensive side; the full suffix↔probe race was not run
- **P151 H-chain-of-thought: Hijacking the Chain-of-Thought Safety Reasoning** [link](https://www.researchgate.net/publication/389130378) · Kuo et al. 2025 · covered: a **universal transferable** attack on LRMs (o1/o3, R1, Gemini-2.0-Flash-Thinking) — chain-of-thought substitution, refusal 98%→<2% ← a prompt-level chain-of-thought hijack that transfers across reasoning models (NOT transfer of an activation-level evasion — open)

### MODALITY + ACCESS axes: multimodality, agents, probe-as-attack (NEW axis — almost empty outside text/white)
> Refusal geometry outside text and outside white-box (grey-box probe-as-attack; cross-model transfer of evasion).
- **P101 Safety Geometry Collapse in Multimodal LLMs** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.18104) · 2026 · MODALITY=vision-language · covered: a modality shift **systematically destroys** the text-aligned refusal geometry (not isolated adversarial inputs); adaptive modality-gap correction
- **P102 Do LLMs and VLMs Share Neurons for Inference?** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.19058) · 2026 · CODE+ · MODALITY=vision-language · covered: >50% of top inference neurons are shared LLM↔vision-language (modality-invariant subspace), low-rank transfer ← transfer along the modality axis
- **P103 Refusal Before Decoding (Mechanistic AutoDAN, probe-guided)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.28553) · 2026 · ACCESS=grey-box · covered: refusal is read linearly from intermediate activations BEFORE the answer; **the probe guides the attack** (−72% time, transfer ≥ AutoDAN) ← the probe as a weapon, not just a defense
- **P106 Indirect Prompt Injection in the Wild** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2604.27202) · 2026 · MODALITY=agent · covered: empirics of IPI (1.2B URLs, 15.3K cases); 99% — task-override, 43% jailbreak-framing ← agentic attack surface (outside refusal geometry, systems security: CaMeL/SecAlign/AgentDojo)
- **P112 SARSteer: Safe-Ablated Refusal Steering (audio LALM)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2510.17633) · Lin et al. 2025 (ICML26) · CODE+ (linweiii/SARSteer) · MODALITY=audio · covered: audio **more easily** elicits harm; LLM steering **breaks under audio** (distributional gap) → text-derived refusal vector + safe-space ablation (PCA) against over-refusal (related: taxonomy of audio jailbreaks [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.30031))
- **P123 vision-language Jailbreaks via Jailbreak-Related Representation Shift (JRS-Rem)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.17372) · 2026 · MODALITY=vision-language · covered: **the image shifts the representation toward a separate jailbreak state** (separable from benign and refusal); a **jailbreak direction** = refuse→jailbreak; JRS-Rem removes the shift at inference ← a refusal direction exists in VLMs
- **P124 NullSteer: Null-space Projected Steering (vision-language defense)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.22094) · 2026 · MODALITY=vision-language · covered: the refusal direction in a **subspace orthogonal to benign** (zero perturbation on benign) → safety without over-refusal (MiniGPT-4 −15% ASR) ← porting the null-space/LEACE idea into VLMs
- **P125 JailBound: Internal Safety Boundary of vision-language** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2505.19610) · 2025 · MODALITY=vision-language · ACCESS=white-box→black-box · covered: probing the safety boundary by layer (100% acc), joint image+text attack across the boundary; white-box ASR 94% (LLaMA-3.2), transfer to GPT-4o 75%
- **P152 Encoding-Behavior Dissociation in VLMs** [link](https://openreview.net/forum?id=GGFjPyO3mZ) · 2026 · MODALITY=vision-language · LOCUS=cone-subspace · covered: **the first measurement of the DIMENSIONALITY of the safety representation in VLMs** — encoding harmful↔benign requires **~50 PCA components** (vs 1 in text-LLMs), the signal spans all positions/heads, **robust to iterative ablation** (vs Gemma-IT collapse after ~11); BUT behaviorally — a single **DIM threshold** (benign refusal 52→98%); per-image/universal PGD evasion 98.4%/96.9% (ε=8/255), **cross-model transfer weak ≤11%** ← dimensionality outside text (encoding-dim, not a RepInd cone) + evasion transfers weakly (cross-model ≤11%)
- **P131 Jailbreaking MLLMs using Multi-Clip Video (MCV)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2606.02111) · 2026 · MODALITY=video · covered: video is **more dangerous than images**; the more diverse the clips, the more the model **internally represents the input as LESS harmful** → frame-filter defense (related: VSE-Eval benchmark [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2505.16643), SPTV safety-proximal frames [link](https://openreview.net/pdf?id=qVMtipJxgE)) ← closes the previously empty MODALITY=video cell
- **P135 OmniSteer: Omni-Safety under Cross-Modality Conflict** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.10161) · 2026 · MODALITY=audio/vision-language (cross-modal) · covered: a **unified cross-modal "golden" refusal vector via SVD** (text/audio/image/video), layer-wise adaptive steering by adapters; RSR 69.9→91.2% ← the closest thing to a refusal SUBSPACE in audio + transfer across modalities
- **P136 Acoustic Interference (AIA, audio mechanism)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.18168) · 2026 · MODALITY=audio · covered: **benign-by-content audio with paralinguistics = a universal trigger**; the mechanism — **inference path drift** that steers the internal representation **out of the safety subspace** (interpretable ALS patterns) ← an audio analog of "shifting out of the cone"

### POLARITY axis: geometry of OVER-refusal (NEW branch from RQ2 — a separate structure)
> Excessive refusal is NOT the same direction as harmful refusal: task-dependent, higher-dimensional.
- **P107 Over-Refusal and Representation Subspaces** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.27518) · 2026 · POLARITY=over-refusal · covered: harmful refusal = a task-agnostic single direction (early-mid layers); **over-refusal = a task-dependent, higher-dimensional subspace** within task-identity clusters → global ablation misses, a task-conditioned steering is needed (LLaMA-3.1-8B)
- **P108 Beyond Over-Refusal (MS-XSB diagnostics)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2510.08158) · 2025 · POLARITY=over-refusal · covered: a scenario-based multi-turn over-refusal benchmark + post-hoc mitigation (ignore-word/rephrase/attention-steer) without weight access

### STAGE axis: harmful-finetuning, direction drift, transfer through compression (NEW axis)
> Where in the lifecycle the mechanism lives/moves/breaks. Previously everything sat at inference-time.
- **P109 Safety Anchor: Defending Harmful Fine-tuning (Geometric Bottleneck)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.05995) · 2026 · STAGE=finetune-time · covered: harmful-finetuning exploits **parameter redundancy**; the defense ports to the unembedding "bottleneck" (SBR), a single safety-anchor holds safety; refusal⊥benign-reasoning
- **P110 Refusal in Compressed Models (AIRD)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2504.04215) · 2025 · STAGE=compress · covered: **pruning SHIFTS the source of the refusal direction (loses safety), quantization PRESERVES it**; AIRD restores it ← transfer along the compression axis
- **P111 Analysing the Safety Pitfalls of Steering Vectors** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.24543) · 2026 · covered: CAA steering vectors overlap with the refusal direction (bimodal cosine) → ±50% ASR; a mechanistic explanation of "steering externalities" (related to P21)
- **P116 RefusalGuard: Geometry-Preserving Fine-Tuning** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.01913) · 2026 · STAGE=finetune-time · covered: finetune degrades safety via **3 effects** — (i) directional drift, (ii) geometric distortion, (iii) interference of task↔safety updates; the defense preserves the geometry
- **P117 Anchoring Refusal Direction (ProCon, drift under IFT)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2509.06795) · Du et al. 2025 · STAGE=finetune-time · covered: the r-direction **gradually drifts** under instruction-FT (stronger in deep layers, max after the 1st epoch) → projection-constraint anchor ← echoes P69/P79 ("the circuit relocates")
- **P126 Tracing Persona Vectors Through Pretraining** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.13329) · 2026 · STAGE=pretraining · covered: persona directions **form very early** (0.22% of tokens), persist → post-training **elicits rather than installs**; base vectors steer the post-trained model ← a developmental axis (DYNAMICS=across-training)
- **P127 When Should We Introduce Safety Interventions During Pretraining?** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2601.07087) · 2026 · STAGE=pretraining · covered: timing of safety data in pretraining (refusal "natively" in the corpus); introduction after 20–60% — safer and more robust to downstream-FT
- **P134 DeepRefusal: Probabilistically Ablating Refusal Direction** [pdf](https://aclanthology.org/2025.findings-emnlp.956.pdf) · 2025 (EMNLP) · STAGE=finetune-time+DYNAMICS=across-generation · CODE+ (YuanBoXie/DeepRefusal) · covered: the defense **randomly ablates the refusal direction across layers AND token depths** during FT → the model learns to **rebuild refusal from a jailbreak state** at any depth; −95% ASR ← confirms that safety is distributed over token-depth (echo of P133/shallow-safety P30)

> **Note (revision 8/9) — related findings without a separate line:** LAT compactifies refusal into a low-dim subspace (Abbas et al. 2025), changing transferability; video-diffusion refusal vectors (Facchiano 2025) — an extension along the MODALITY axis; RED (Refusal-Escape Directions, [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.08878)) and Refusal-Trajectory ([pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.02958)) — an operator/spatiotemporal refinement of the mechanism (the terminal position carries an attenuated signal → hits the probe on the last token); machine-unlearning safety geometry (RePAIR / Geometric-disentanglement Unlearning) — a neighboring STAGE branch.

### LOCUS axis = mixture-of-experts-expert (NEW unit of analysis)
> Between "neuron" and "weights": the mixture-of-experts expert as a unit for counting safety mechanisms.
- **P113 SAFEx: Safety-Critical Experts in mixture-of-experts** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2506.17368) · 2025 (NeurIPS25) · LOCUS=mixture-of-experts-expert · covered: **positional vulnerability** — safety is concentrated in experts; decomposition into **HCDG (harm detection) vs HRCG (response control)** (echo of harm≠refusal P90); disabling 12/6144 experts → −22% refusal
- **P114 Understanding Safety-Sensitive Expert Behavior (RASET)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.29708) · 2026 · LOCUS=mixture-of-experts-expert · covered: is refusal via **routing to safety experts** OR within topic experts? RASET (router-agnostic) edits selected experts with a fixed router (related to RASA [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.04448)); cf. P80 (refusal ≠ routing signal → role of attention)

### Axes from edge-discovery (consolidated)
> Proposed while building the edges, then merged by the consolidation round (the four former boolean flags collapsed into these). The map is still thin here: high novelty, but also higher methodological risk.

**D1. DYNAMICS — how the refusal mechanism changes over time (`across-generation` within one decode, `across-training` across checkpoints; merges the former temporal + training-dynamics flags)**
- **P118 Step-Wise Refusal Dynamics (AR + Diffusion LM)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.02600) · 2026 · DYNAMICS=across-generation+ARCHITECTURE=diffusion-language-model · covered: a step-wise internal refusal signal (SRI) along generation; jailbreak = **incomplete internal recovery** of the trajectory; works for **diffusion language model** too
- **P119 Inference-Time Vulnerability Beyond Shallow Safety (generation trajectories)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2606.04778) · 2026 · DYNAMICS=across-generation · covered: a PCA-plane refuse↔comply; injection at step t **sharply steers the trajectory** to comply; bidirectional sensitivity → training augmentation
- **P133 Refusal Trajectory / SALO (spatiotemporal detector)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.02958) · 2026 · DYNAMICS=across-generation · covered: refusal is **not a static direction but a trajectory over layer-tokens** (3 phases: anchor→onset→terminal); **the terminal position carries an ATTENUATED signal** (hits the probe on the last token), the upstream pattern **survives GCG** → the SALO detector (terminal token attenuated, emphasis on the last-token probe)
- **P120 Dynamic Adversarial FT Reorganizes Refusal Geometry** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2604.27019) · 2026 · DYNAMICS=across-training · covered: adversarial-FT (R2D2) **relocates the admissible carrier** (late→early layer), and does **NOT expand dimensionality** into independent fail-closed paths ← important for the count (B): "more holes" ≠ "more dimensions" (developmental: OLMo — directions become usable only from mid-pretrain, [link](https://www.lesswrong.com/posts/nKfmimhTKCsDM2Hwh/harmfulness-directions-in-olmo-1))

**D2. ARCHITECTURE — dense / mixture-of-experts / state-space-model / diffusion-language-model**
- Interp tools (CAA / tuned-lens / ELK) **mostly transfer** to Mamba/RWKV (Belrose et al., [link](https://aisafetypapers.substack.com/p/mamba-interpretability-neel-nandas)), but recurrent-state brings its own vulnerabilities to contextual distractors; mixture-of-experts experts — the mixture-of-experts-expert cluster; diffusion language models — below. ← refusal cones/RepInd outside the dense transformer have barely been measured
- **P128 DiJA: The Devil behind the mask (dLLM jailbreak)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2507.11097) · 2025 · ARCHITECTURE=diffusion-language-model · CODE+ · covered: the first systematic jailbreak of diffusion language model — interleaved mask-text exploits **bidirectionality + parallel decoding** (no dynamic filtering); up to 100% ASR (Dream/LLaDA)
- **P129 TrajHijack: Re-Mask and Redirect (dLLM)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2604.08557) · 2026 · ARCHITECTURE=diffusion-language-model+DYNAMICS=across-generation · covered: a trajectory-level attack (re-mask committed refusal tokens + affirmative prefix), 74–98% ASR; **Defense Inversion Effect** — the A2D defense makes the model MORE vulnerable (89.9% vs 76.1%) ← dLLM defenses do not transfer from AR (related to A2D [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2509.23286), step-wise SGD-steering [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.13043))
- **P132 Refusal in LLMs is an Affine Function (ACE on RWKV/SSM)** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2411.09003) · 2024 · ARCHITECTURE=state-space-model · covered: affine concept editing unifies ablation+CAA; **works on RWKV-v5 (Hermes Eagle)**, where pure directional-ablation produces gibberish → the refusal direction exists outside the dense transformer too; porting tools to Mamba: IOI circuit [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2407.14008), ROME-facts [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2404.03646), CAA/tuned-lens [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2404.05971) (refusal steering weaker) ← closes the previously empty cell of refusal geometry in SSM/RWKV

**D3. Trigger-conditioned behaviour (the RQ23 row) — behavior under a trigger**
- **P121 Sleeper Agents** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2401.05566) · Hubinger et al. 2024 (Anthropic) · RQ23 · covered: backdoors **survive safety-training** (RL/SFT/adversarial); adversarial-training **hides** rather than removes; defection is **linearly detected** (>99% AUROC defection-probe) ← a probe on conditional behavior
- **P122 Triggers Hijack Language Circuits** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.10382) · 2026 · RQ23 · covered: triggers **co-opt existing circuits** (overlap with language-heads) rather than build separate ones; form early (7.5–25% of depth) — a mechanistic analysis of the backdoor

**D4. non-unique-direction (axis id IDENTIFIABILITY) — is the "direction/mechanism" unique (theory of counting)**
- **P130 Provably Extracting Features from a General Superposition** [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2512.15987) · 2025 · non-unique-direction · covered: in superposition, features are **identifiable ⇔ the response functions are NONlinear**; linearity is the only obstacle ← a theoretical basis for counting: "counting mechanisms" is correct only with a nonlinear criterion (= RepInd P18, nonlinear refusal P83); related to the spectral theory of superposition [pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.02224)

---

## 2-bis. Remapping: which OLD papers populate the new axes (audit of axis completeness)

> Phase A. Many already-entered papers belong to the new axes but were not attached there. After
> remapping, the new axes are filled more strongly than they seemed in rev.8 — and it becomes visible which cells are really empty.

| New axis → value | Old papers that "relocate"/cross-tag here | New (P101+) |
|---|---|---|
| **MODALITY=vision-language** | P65 (survey LLM/vision-language) | P101, P102, P123, P124, P125 |
| **MODALITY=video** | — | P131 |
| **MODALITY=audio/agent** | — | P112, P135, P136 (audio); P106 (agent) |
| **ACCESS=black-box** | P02, P09, P10, P11, P12, P13, P54 (profiling) | P106 |
| **ACCESS=grey-box** | P29 (subspace-rerouting), P38 (obfuscation) | P103 |
| **STAGE=pretraining** | P95 (univ neurons emerge) | P126, P127 |
| **STAGE=finetune-time** | P30, P31, P39, P40, P52, P55, P91 | P109, P116, P117 |
| **LOCUS=token** | P27 (chat tokens), P30 (first tokens) | — |
| **LOCUS=cone-subspace** | P18, P19, P29, P84 | P107 (over-cone) |
| **LOCUS=sparse-autoencoder-feature** | P32, P33, P34, P35, P36 | P94 |
| **LOCUS=neuron** | P37, P51–P55, P95 | — |
| **LOCUS=circuit-head** | P25, P27, P79 | — |
| **LOCUS=mixture-of-experts-expert** | P80 | P113, P114 |
| **LOCUS=weights** | P17 (rank-1), P53 (weight-edit), P57 (LEACE), P68 (SRA) | — |
| **BEHAVIOR=harmfulness** | P32 | P90, P91 |
| **BEHAVIOR=over-refusal** | P61, P62, P63 (benchmarks!) | P107, P108 |
| **BEHAVIOR=persona-traits/deception/emergent-misalignment/truth** | P71–P78, P72, P73–P75, P84 | P88, P89, P115, P137, P138, P140 |
| **BEHAVIOR=code-style** | — | P139 |
| **LOCUS=cone-subspace (cross-behavior)** | P74 (low-rank emergent-misalignment) | P137 |
| **POLARITY=over-refusal** | P21, P61, P62, P63 | P107, P108 |
| **DYNAMICS=across-generation** | P30/P31 (depth), P86 (trajectory), P99/P100 (chain-of-thought) | P118, P119, P133, P134, P19 (count×layer) |
| **DYNAMICS=across-training** | P69, P79 ("the circuit relocates"), P91, P95 | P117, P120, P126 |
| **ARCHITECTURE≠dense** | P35 (Mamba-sparse-autoencoders), P66 (Dense→mixture-of-experts), P80 | P113, P114, P118, P128, P129, P132 |
| **non-unique-direction** | P18 (RepInd), P19 (collapse-to-knob + count by layer), P83 (nonlin.), P96/P98 (epiphenomena), P107 (n80 over) | P115, P130 |

**Verdict on the axes (phase A — completeness/consistency):**
- **Consistency:** the remapping **created no conflicts** — no paper falls into two mutually exclusive values of one axis (white↔black etc.). Multi-tagging (P29 = grey+cone; P54 = black+neuron+transfer) is legitimate, not a contradiction.
- **Completeness (after the rev.12 load):** two more of the three remaining cells were filled. **(1) audio** — now a cross-modal refusal subspace via SVD (P135 OmniSteer) + an audio mechanism of "shifting out of the safety subspace" (P136 AIA); the only thing still undone is **explicit RepInd/multi-cone in audio** (there is a subspace, but independence of directions is not proven). **(2) cone count × layer/generation** — turned out to be **already in the catalog**: P19 gives strict refusal cores by layer (591/517/421) + shrinkage of the universal core deeper, P107 — n80 for over-refusal; cross-tagged onto non-unique-direction + DYNAMICS=across-generation. **(3) the ONLY truly empty cell (NOT COVERED):** **count/RepInd of refusal cones in diffusion/Mamba/mixture-of-experts** — attacks exist (P128/P129/P132/P113/P114), but **the cone dimensionality and independence of directions outside dense text have not been measured by anyone**.
- **Key consistency:** P130 (identifiability ⇔ nonlinearity) + P18 (RepInd catches nonlinearity) + P83 (refusal is nonlinear) + P120 (FT relocates, does not expand) **add up to one thesis**: a correct "count of independent mechanisms" must (a) use a nonlinear criterion (RepInd), (b) separate carrier relocation from the emergence of a new dimension. This resolves the apparent contradiction "many directions (P18) ↔ one switch (P19)".

---

## 3. Coverage rollup (all axes)

The canonical coverage view: one row per axis, every axis an equal dimension of the
tensor — no axis or pair is privileged. `values covered / total` = how many of the axis's
values have at least one covered cell; `densest value` = the value carrying the most
covered cells; `empty values` = values no covered cell touches yet (gaps along that axis).

| axis | values covered / total | densest value (cells) | empty values (gaps along this axis) |
|---|---|---|---|
| RQ | 21 / 24 | RQ1 (10) | RQ11, RQ12, RQ16 |
| LOCUS | 8 / 8 | direction (61) | - |
| MECH | 6 / 6 | directions (56) | - |
| CODE | 3 / 3 | not-released (61) | - |
| MODELS | 2 / 2 | multi (96) | - |
| TRANSFER | 4 / 4 | not-tested (82) | - |
| MODALITY | 6 / 6 | text (92) | - |
| ACCESS | 3 / 3 | white-box (91) | - |
| STAGE | 5 / 6 | inference-time (90) | rlhf-alignment |
| BEHAVIOR | 7 / 9 | refusal (76) | over-refusal, deception |
| POLARITY | 3 / 3 | under-refusal (65) | - |
| DYNAMICS | 3 / 3 | static (90) | - |
| ARCHITECTURE | 4 / 4 | dense (94) | - |
| IDENTIFIABILITY | 2 / 2 | no (94) | - |

### 3-lens. Reading lens (optional): RQ × coverage-status

One reading lens among equals (the RQ axis read against each question's coverage status);
it privileges no axis — any axis could be read this way.

| RQ (question) | Status | Who closed it (key) | Gap / what's left |
|---|---|---|---|
| RQ1 Does a refusal direction exist | **DONE** | P17 | — |
| RQ2 How many directions / geometry (cones, dim) | **DONE** | P18, P19, P82 (SOM), P83 (nonlin.) | cone dim vs number of arms-race generations — not linked |
| RQ3 Suffix mechanism (suppression vs activation, hijack) | **DONE** | P17, P26, P27, P25, **P90 (harm≠refusal: jailbreak suppresses refusal, not harmfulness)** | the link to probe-neuron mismatch is not shown |
| RQ4 Does a probe detect harm | **DONE** | P32, P42, P44, P45 | — |
| RQ5 Evasion of probe/latent monitors | **DONE** | P38 (+P39/P40 are bypassed) | robustness of the evasion under model change |
| RQ6 Defense (input / latent / multi-layer) | **PARTIAL** | P39, P41, P42, P47 | NOT COVERED: a public adaptive multi-layer defense (P41 code is closed) |
| RQ7 Count of harm-schemas/neurons (min/max) | **PARTIAL** | P51, P52, P33 (hydra), P56 (ID), P18 ("a limited number"), P82 (16 SOM) | NOT COVERED: **a rigorous estimate of the number of INDEPENDENT mechanisms** (arms-race as an idea is taken by P92/P93, but counting through it — NO) |
| RQ8 Transfer across models (suffix/dir/feature/neuron) | **CONTESTED/PARTIAL** | transfer: P66 (dir), P67 (cross-lingual), P22 (SV), P54 (neurons cross-arch), **P84 (truth cones)**, P81 (jailbreaks=representational proximity), **P94 (crosscoders shared features)**, P95 (1–5% univ. neurons) · refute: P69+P79 (lab-specific) | NOT COVERED: transfer of **CONES shown for truth (P84), but NOT for refusal**; transfer of **probe evasion — OPEN**; the single refusal direction — disputed P66/P67 ↔ P69/P79 |
| RQ9 sparse-autoencoders decomposition of refusal (core/tail, redundancy) | **DONE** | P19, P32, P33 | link redundancy ↔ arms-race generations |
| RQ10 Attacks stronger than GCG | **DONE/moving** | P03, P06, P10 | — |
| RQ11 Several TYPES of refusal (taxonomy) | **DONE** | P19, P61, P63, P62 | — |
| RQ12 Shallow vs deep alignment | **DONE** | P30, P31 | — |
| RQ13 Benchmarks/evaluation | **DONE** | P58, P59, P60 | — |
| RQ14 Other directions/circuits (sycophancy/deception/emergent-misalignment/traits): do they exist? do they transfer? | **PARTIAL→DONE for directions** | P70–P78, **P88 Persona Vectors**, P89 | NOT COVERED: trait directions are taken (P88); **cones/RepInd/arms-race/count for NON-refusal — not done** |
| RQ15 Refusal in reasoning/chain-of-thought models: one direction? cones? does the picture transfer? | **OPEN/weak** | P99, P100 (refusal distributed over chain-of-thought, dual-signal) | NOT COVERED: **cones/RepInd/count/transfer/probe evasion in reasoning models — almost untouched** ← a fresh axis |
| RQ16 Predict-control: a feature predicts ≠ controls | **PARTIAL (filling fast)** | P78, P96, P97, P98 | NOT COVERED: a rigorous necessity test (silence) for the refusal probe specifically — not done |
| RQ17 Geometry of OVER-refusal (POLARITY) — a separate direction? how many dim? | **PARTIAL (new)** | P107 (task-dependent, higher-dim), P108 | NOT COVERED: **cones/RepInd/count/transfer for over-refusal** — not done; link to under-refusal cones |
| RQ18 Transfer of safety geometry across MODALITY (text→vision-language/audio/video) | **PARTIAL (new)** | vision-language P101/P123/P124/P125; audio P112/P135/P136; video P131; cross-modal SVD-subspace P135 | NOT COVERED: **RepInd/multi-cone/count in vision-language/audio/video** — empty (there is a subspace, independence not proven) |
| RQ19 STAGE: emergence/drift/transfer of the direction (pretrain→finetune→compress) | **PARTIAL (new)** | P126/P127 (emerges in pretraining), P109, P110, P116, P117 (drift) | NOT COVERED: **how the NUMBER/cone of mechanisms changes along training/compression** — not done |
| RQ20 ACCESS: probe-as-attack (grey-box), degradation under access change | **PARTIAL (new)** | P103 (Mechanistic AutoDAN), P38 (obfuscation) | NOT COVERED: **co-design of attack↔probe as a meter** (grey-box arms-race) — not formalized |
| ⚗️RQ21 DYNAMICS=across-generation: dynamics of refusal/cone along generation steps | **PARTIAL (new)** | P118, P119, P133 (trajectory/SALO), P134 (depth-ablation) | NOT COVERED: **how the NUMBER/cone of mechanisms changes along decoding** — not done; the terminal token is attenuated (P133) |
| ⚗️RQ22 ARCHITECTURE: cones/RepInd/count outside dense (mixture-of-experts/Mamba/diffusion) | **PARTIAL (new)** | mixture-of-experts P113/P114; diffusion P118/P128/P129; **RWKV/SSM P132** (ACE); P35/Mamba-interp | NOT COVERED: **the number/cone of refusal mechanisms in SSM/diffusion** is not measured (only a single direction exists); dLLM defenses do not transfer (P129) |
| ⚗️RQ23 BACKDOOR/conditional: geometry of trigger-behavior, count | **PARTIAL** | P121 (defection-probe), P122 (hijack circuits) | NOT COVERED: **cones/count/transfer for conditional behaviors** — not done |
| ⚗️RQ24 non-unique-direction: is the direction/cone unique? how many of them "really"? | **OPEN (theory + count by layer)** | P130 (ident. ⇔ nonlinearity), P18 (RepInd), P19 (cores 591/517/421 by layer), P107 (n80), P115/P98 | NOT COVERED: **apply a nonlinear criterion to counting refusal mechanisms OUTSIDE dense text** (diffusion/mixture-of-experts/audio) |

**Bottom line:** RQ1–RQ5, RQ9–RQ13 — closed. Clean gaps: **RQ7** (count of independent mechanisms — arms-race as an *idea* is taken by P92/P93, what's open is "the race as a **meter** of number", not the race itself), **RQ15** (refusal in reasoning/chain-of-thought — a fresh axis, almost empty). **RQ6** has narrowed (P85/P86). **RQ8** is disputed + cones transferred only for truth (P84); crosscoders (P94) and universal neurons (P95) were added. **RQ16** (predict-control) is filling fast (P96–P98), but the necessity test for the refusal probe was not done. **RQ14** — trait directions are taken by P88.

## §3-bis. Required-but-uncovered (field-requested) and dig-candidates

Tensor points with zero papers: empty but coverable points a researcher could fill
with a paper. These are the map's `required-uncovered` cells —
**authored in the `fieldmap-uncovered` block** (the source of truth) and shown red on
the coverage map. They are a status of the CELL, computed by the map alone and
**independent of the edges**. The block now authors **88** such full-tensor gap points:
the **8 authored core gaps** detailed in Group A below, plus **76 full-coordinate
materializations of the field's future-work targets** (every edge target that lands on
an empty point becomes one authored red cell; its note is the future-work idea that
asks for it), plus **4 greenfield-anchored gaps** authored by the instrument's own
greenfield pass (the askable cones the field only has a single direction for — in audio,
non-refusal behaviors, diffusion and mixture-of-experts; the red-gap targets of the
`source=greenfield` questions in `FIELD_MAP_edges.md`). Group A/B below detail the 8 core
gaps and the analysis-only dig-candidates; §3-ter and §3-quater then enumerate the red
cells by their full coordinate (no axis or pair privileged).

**Group A - the 8 authored core required-uncovered points.** Each is a FULL tensor point with no catalog paper (its coords are the full coordinate spec of the paper that would cover it); the experiment column is the note that ships with the cell — what would cover it. (The other 76 authored red cells are the materialized future-work targets, listed in the `fieldmap-uncovered` block.)

| Gap cell | Experiment that would cover it (the cell's note) | Also asked by future-work (context only) |
|---|---|---|
| `weights__training` | Study weight-level training as a refusal carrier: measure how training-time weight updates and curriculum or safety-neuron interventions (F13) move, erase or restore the refusal direction and whether that direction and geometry transfer across families and scales (F1). Test whether a geometry-preserving finetune defends it against finetuning-induced safety degradation (F11) and black-box transferable jailbreaks (F15), whether the weight-space analysis extends to non-refusal behaviors such as truth, sycophancy and persona (F6) and to mechanisms of harmful behavior beyond the refusal direction (F28), and standardize the layer-targeted steering used to read and restore it (F24). | 11 edges (P109 redundancy, P127/P126 pretraining curriculum, P30/P31 alignment-depth, P73/P74 emergent misalignment, P71, P14, P29, P90). |
| `mixture-of-experts-expert__directions` | Extract refusal directions/cones per mixture-of-experts expert and test whether refusal is carried by routing vs within-expert directions, then transfer to a full-size mixture-of-experts. | P80 (refusal signal ≠ expert-routing; full-size mixture-of-experts), P113 (auto-discovery of expert groups, routing fallback), P114 (router-agnostic). |
| `neuron__training` | Track the emergence and stability of safety neurons across pretraining/finetuning checkpoints; does the universal-neuron share change with training and scale. | P51 (evolution/stability of safety-neuron emergence; realizers P95/P126/P127). |
| `cone-subspace__training` | Measure how the refusal cone drifts, distorts or relocates across instruction/harmful finetuning, and whether the cone dimensionality itself changes. | P116 (drift/distortion/interference), P117 (drift sharper in early/deep layers), P120 (relocation, not a general theory). |
| `circuit-head__directions` | Integrate the representation and circuit views: determine whether a protective, jailbreak-resistant refusal circuit exists and what makes the removable latent circuit removable, testing whether that circuit and its geometry transfer across model families and scales (F1), whether it withstands black-box transferable jailbreaks (F15), and how it relates to mechanisms of harmful behavior beyond the refusal direction (F28). | P25 (integrate repr + circuit; scale-invariant), P40 (mechanism of the removable latent circuit), P33 (jailbreak-resistant prompts → protective circuits). |
| `cone-subspace__directions` (text · under-refusal · RQ2) | Extract the refusal cone optimally rather than as an existence-proof: current work only shows a refusal direction/cone exists with heuristic extraction, so derive the refusal subspace with a principled non-heuristic method, measure its true dimensionality, and test whether the cone (not just a single direction) transfers across models. | Broad `MECH=directions` future-work pressure (P18/P29 existence-proof → optimal extraction; cross-model cone transfer open). This is a DIFFERENT full point from the covered `cone-subspace × directions` papers (TRANSFER=not-tested, MODELS=multi). |
| `direction__directions` (MODALITY=agent · RQ8) | Test the refusal direction in the agentic setting: extract the inference-time refusal direction inside a tool-using agent loop and measure whether single-direction ablation still removes refusal across multi-step agent trajectories, and whether it transfers across agent backbones. | Broad transfer-to-agents future-work pressure; the only agentic `direction × directions` paper (P106) is black-box prompt-injection empirics, so this white-box refusal-direction-geometry point in the agent setting is empty. |
| `direction__directions` (BEHAVIOR=emergent-misalignment · RQ14) | Isolate an emergent-misalignment direction against refusal: extract a linear direction for emergent misalignment, test whether it is independent of or entangled with the refusal direction, and whether steering along it induces broad misalignment. | RQ14 other-directions pressure (sycophancy/deception/emergent-misalignment); a distinct full point from the refusal-behavior core. |

**Group B - analysis-only refinements we did NOT elevate to red cells.** Each is still a FULL empty tensor point (a gap is a point like any other - no privileged pair), but it sits one single-axis step from an already-covered cell (e.g. running the cone apparatus on a new ARCHITECTURE, while the rest of the coordinate stays on the covered `cone-subspace × directions` papers), so we judged it a dig-candidate rather than authoring it into `fieldmap-uncovered`; they stay here as our own follow-ups.

| Gap cell | Experiment that would cover it | Why coverable |
|---|---|---|
| `cone-subspace__directions__new-architecture` | Run the RDO + RepInd cone extraction (P18) on a diffusion language model, a state-space model and an audio language model and report cone dimensionality and direction independence against the dense-text baseline. | The cone/RepInd apparatus exists (P18) and attacks already reach these architectures (P128/P129/P132/P113/P114); only the cone count outside dense text is unmeasured (line 503, RQ22/RQ18). |
| `circuit-head__attention__cross-family` | Extract the refusal/affirmation-head hijack signature on family A, map it onto family B, and test whether the same head-level hijack predicts jailbreak success cross-family. | Heads and the hijack mechanism are known within a model (P25/P27/P122); their transfer across families has not been studied (section 4 gap 3). |
| `sparse-autoencoder-feature__sparse-autoencoders__mechanism-count` | Iterate suffix-attack vs feature-ablation rounds and count how many independent sparse-autoencoder features must be removed before refusal collapses irreversibly. | Redundant 'hydra' features (P33) and the arms-race idea (P92/P93) both exist; nobody has used the race depth as a count of independent mechanisms (RQ7, section 4 gap 1). |
| `cone-subspace__directions__over-refusal` | Apply the P18 cone/RepInd apparatus to over-refusal (P107/P108) and test whether over-refusal occupies an independent cone from under-refusal. | Over-refusal already has a measured single direction (P107) and a benchmark (P108); its cone structure and link to the under-refusal cone are open (RQ17). |

## §3-ter. What each cell reflects

| Cell (full coordinate) | What the cell holds |
|---|---|
| `token__behavior` | Adversarial suffixes and jailbreak prompts at the token level, read through the behavioral lens (ASR / refusal-rate); the GCG family and its successors live here. |
| `direction__directions` | Refusal as a single linear direction in activation space, found and manipulated with the directions lens (difference-of-means, directional ablation, activation addition, weight abliteration). |
| `cone-subspace__directions` | Multi-dimensional refusal cones and low-rank safety subspaces read with the directions lens, beyond a single vector (including over-refusal and cross-modal subspaces). |
| `direction__training` | How the refusal direction emerges, drifts and relocates across the training lifecycle (pretraining → supervised-finetuning / rlhf-alignment → harmful-finetune → compression), read with training-time interventions. |
| `sparse-autoencoder-feature__sparse-autoencoders` | Sparse-autoencoder decomposition of refusal into a small core plus a redundant tail of features, read with the sparse-autoencoders lens. |
| `neuron__neurons` | Individual safety neurons and small neuron sets that carry refusal, read at the neuron level. |
| `circuit-head__attention` | Refusal/affirmation attention heads and circuits, and the suffix-as-attention-hijack mechanism, read with the attention lens. |
| `circuit-head__neurons` | Refusal/safety circuits read at the circuit-neuron granularity, including trigger-conditioned (backdoor) behavior. |
| `mixture-of-experts-expert__neurons` | Refusal localised to mixture-of-experts experts, read at the mixture-of-experts-expert granularity. |
| `token__attention` | Token-level attention reading of refusal: how prompt/suffix tokens steer attention. |
| `token__training` | Token-level safety training: where in the token sequence alignment lives and how deep it goes. |
| `weights__directions` | Weight-level removal of the refusal direction: edits baked permanently into parameters. |

Test whether a geometry-preserving finetune defends it against finetuning-induced safety degradation (F11) and black-box transferable jailbreaks (F15), whether the weight-space analysis extends to non-refusal behaviors such as truth, sycophancy and persona (F6) and to mechanisms of harmful behavior beyond the refusal direction (F28), and standardize the layer-targeted steering used to read and restore it (F24). |
