| `RQ=RQ19;LOCUS=cone-subspace;MECH=training;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=finetune-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=across-training;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | Measure how the refusal cone drifts, distorts or relocates across instruction/harmful finetuning, and whether the cone dimensionality itself changes. |
| `RQ=RQ6;LOCUS=circuit-head;MECH=directions;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=yes;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | Integrate the representation and circuit views: determine whether a protective, jailbreak-resistant refusal circuit exists and what makes the removable latent circuit removable, testing whether that circuit and its geometry transfer across model families and scales (F1), whether it withstands black-box transferable jailbreaks (F15), and how it relates to mechanisms of harmful behavior beyond the refusal direction (F28). |
| `RQ=RQ2;LOCUS=cone-subspace;MECH=directions;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | Extract the refusal cone optimally rather than as an existence-proof: current work only shows a refusal direction/cone exists with heuristic extraction, so derive the refusal subspace with a principled non-heuristic method, measure its true dimensionality, and test whether the cone (not just a single direction) transfers across models. |
| `RQ=RQ8;LOCUS=direction;MECH=directions;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=agent;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | Test the refusal direction in the agentic setting: extract the inference-time refusal direction inside a tool-using agent loop and measure whether single-direction ablation still removes refusal across multi-step agent trajectories, and whether it transfers across agent backbones. |
| `RQ=RQ14;LOCUS=direction;MECH=directions;CODE=released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=emergent-misalignment;POLARITY=neutral;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | Isolate an emergent-misalignment direction against refusal: extract a linear direction for emergent misalignment, test whether it is independent of or entangled with the refusal direction, and whether steering along it induces broad misalignment. |
| `RQ=RQ4;LOCUS=direction;MECH=training;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | synthetic boundary-data \| AlphaEvolve auto-search \| robustness to a direct attack on the classifier not checked; probe integrated → may be worse \| intent linearly separable; specificity drops on OOD-benign → deploy calibration |
| `RQ=RQ6;LOCUS=direction;MECH=training;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | Take the CSS+NAAT certificate beyond input-token (l0) robustness: test the defense across architectures and scales (F1), extend it to a latent-space certificate that holds against adaptive attacks (F20), and compare it to perturbation defenses such as char/word disruption, randomized selection and paraphrase smoothing (F21). |
| `RQ=RQ1;LOCUS=cone-subspace;MECH=directions;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | Expand the direction registries and close the entangler-leak with a more reliable, automatic extraction of refusal directions and features (F5), and test whether within-model geodesic/nonlinear steering generalizes to a cross-model cone-to-cone map, i.e. whether refusal is erasable linearly or needs nonlinear methods (F2). |
| `RQ=RQ10;LOCUS=direction;MECH=directions;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | Adversarial suffixes overfit to their source model (83% high-impact in-source but poor transfer): learn a masking that keeps the high-impact tokens while reducing source-distribution dependency, improving the suffix and prompt optimizer (F14) and the resulting black-box, cross-model transferability (F15). |
| `RQ=RQ15;LOCUS=direction;MECH=training;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | ACT recovers a refusal direction robust to adaptive attacks and CoT replacement, but the suffix-versus-probe race under reasoning regeneration was never run: study reasoning-model (LRM) safety directly (F31), testing whether prompt-level CoT-hijacks like H-CoT and activation-level evasion transfer to reasoning models and whether ACT's direction survives as a monitor lever. |
| `RQ=RQ14;LOCUS=cone-subspace;MECH=directions;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=sycophancy;POLARITY=neutral;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | causal separation of sycophancy is linear (3 independent directions); nonlinear/RepInd count and a cone for arbitrary behavior are open \| sycophancy in attention-heads, partially overlaps truthful, independently-modulable; the nonlinear structure not exhausted (linear>nonlinear probe) \| complements the readout geometry P137 (low-rank subspaces of non-refusal) and persona P88; paired with P154/P155 (sycophancy cluster); nonlinear/RepInd count open |
| `RQ=RQ1;LOCUS=cone-subspace;MECH=directions;CODE=released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | Establish that the refusal direction is causal rather than correlational (F4) with a standardized, layer-targeted ablation (F24), then test whether multi-shot and multi-turn role-play attacks (F17) bypass it through a different mechanism than the single-turn Arditi/Rimsky conditioning. |
| `RQ=RQ4;LOCUS=cone-subspace;MECH=directions;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | INNSTEER yields an invertible nonlinear map within a single model, but the cross-model cone-to-cone correspondence is open: test whether refusal is erasable linearly or whether such a nonlinear map is required (F2) by transporting the cone across models and measuring whether linear ablation alone suffices. |
| `RQ=RQ1;LOCUS=cone-subspace;MECH=directions;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=harmfulness;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | Move past the existence-proof: map the layer-wise circuit linking the harmfulness representation (t_inst) to the refusal representation (t_post), counting the mechanisms of harmful behavior that lie beyond the single refusal direction (F28) and quantifying the geometric coupling between the under- and over-refusal cones so that mitigating over-refusal (F7) does not silently re-introduce harmful compliance. |
| `RQ=RQ1;LOCUS=cone-subspace;MECH=directions;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=multi;POLARITY=neutral;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | Pin down the read-versus-steer gap where the refusal signal reads and transfers but steering fails to move behavior (F34): run ablations crossed with paraphrase, scale and temperature under a rigorously powered evaluation pipeline with a justified sample size (F25), separating a genuine causal steering failure from underpowered or confounded measurement. |
| `RQ=RQ5;LOCUS=direction;MECH=training;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | Turn the currently empirical, white-box layer-susceptibility result into a standardized, layer-targeted activation-steering method (F24) that bypasses the most susceptible layers without fine-tuning, and test it for certified or provable robustness (F20) against the adaptive and obfuscation attacks whose resistance is so far unproven. |
| `RQ=RQ9;LOCUS=cone-subspace;MECH=directions;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | Use SAE-based feature analysis of refusal (F27) to weigh the risks and benefits of redundant refusal features, and measure how role-play and multi-turn attacks (F17) perturb those features' activations across a conversation, distinguishing robust redundancy from exploitable redundancy. |
| `RQ=RQ21;LOCUS=cone-subspace;MECH=directions;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=across-generation;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | ROI transfer to 70B+; encoded/Base64 inputs \| alignment with the refusal direction does not predict robustness |
| `RQ=RQ10;LOCUS=cone-subspace;MECH=directions;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=black-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | Safety-Net measures scalar vulnerability to jailbreak (best-of-N), NOT the number of independent mechanisms → open \| Safety-Net (margin, Thm2) — a scalar measure; adjacent to the measures P133/P150, but NOT a count of independent mechanisms (A) |
| `RQ=RQ10;LOCUS=direction;MECH=directions;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=yes;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | Build on the exponentiated-gradient relaxation to craft readable adversarial prompts that transfer better than gibberish suffixes: improve the underlying suffix and prompt optimizer (F14) and measure black-box transfer of the readable attacks across models (F15). |
| `RQ=RQ9;LOCUS=cone-subspace;MECH=directions;CODE=not-released;MODELS=single;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | one feature or interaction of many — open \| depth of entanglement of refusal features with capabilities |
| `RQ=RQ18;LOCUS=direction;MECH=directions;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=no;MODALITY=vision-language;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | VLM refusal encoding looks ~50-PC and ablation-robust, but that is PCA variation plus a single behavioral dimension, not a strict representational-independence or causal count: measure the true dimensionality of the refusal cone outside text and the attacks and defenses on it for vision-language models (F8), where cross-model evasion currently transfers at 11% or less. |
| `RQ=RQ1;LOCUS=cone-subspace;MECH=directions;CODE=released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=partial;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | Current extraction is a heuristic existence-proof shown on one model and one example: make it optimal and automatic (F5), for instance via gradient- or SSR-based discovery, and test whether the extracted direction and its geometry transfer across model families and scales (F1) rather than holding only for the demonstrated case. |
| `RQ=RQ13;LOCUS=direction;MECH=training;CODE=released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=black-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | The threat model is truncated (no system-prompt or prefill) and text-only: extend it to multimodal attacks and defenses (F8) and train against a diverse attack set so adversarial training yields generalizable robustness (F23) rather than overfitting to one attack family like R2D2. |
| `RQ=RQ10;LOCUS=direction;MECH=training;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=black-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | Static heuristics break on frontier models, a generalization gap that only widens with scale where fine-tuning merely delays the failure and adaptive variants reappear: measure robustness across attacker and defender budgets and model scale (F18) and pair it with probe-based monitoring whose OOD generalization is tested (F19). |
| `RQ=RQ6;LOCUS=direction;MECH=training;CODE=released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | Turn the empirical denoising and paraphrase smoothing defense into a certified one: prove a lower-bound accuracy and a certificate on the probability filter (F20), and benchmark it against the family of inference-time perturbation defenses such as smoothing, paraphrase and randomization (F21), accounting for the computational burden it imposes on the attacker. |
| `RQ=RQ24;LOCUS=cone-subspace;MECH=directions;CODE=released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=yes` | diverse targets / RL-judge reward → find MORE independent mechanisms \| do not rule out yet-undiscovered regions mediating refusal |
| `RQ=RQ1;LOCUS=direction;MECH=training;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | No study evaluates inference-time perturbation defenses against a training-derived refusal direction: test whether perplexity filtering, paraphrasing and self-reminder prompts degrade or preserve the white-box refusal signal, and whether smoothing and randomization defenses (F21) blunt direction-based jailbreaks without harming benign refusal. |

| `RQ=RQ22;LOCUS=cone-subspace;MECH=directions;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=mixture-of-experts;IDENTIFIABILITY=yes` | Extract a per-expert refusal cone in a mixture-of-experts model and test whether refusal dimensionality is concentrated in 'safety experts' (P80/P113) or distributed; existing MoE work (P114) studies routing vs within-expert at the neuron level, not the cone. |
| `RQ=RQ18;LOCUS=cone-subspace;MECH=directions;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=audio;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | Extract a multi-DIMENSIONAL refusal cone (not a single direction) inside a large audio-language model and measure its dimensionality, as P152 did for vision-language; current audio work (P135/P136) only recovers a single 'golden' direction. |
| `RQ=RQ24;LOCUS=cone-subspace;MECH=directions;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=truth;POLARITY=neutral;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=yes` | Apply the refusal counting/non-uniqueness apparatus (RQ24) to a non-refusal behavior: extract the truth subspace, measure its dimensionality and whether many directions give the same behavioral shift, paralleling P84's truth cones. |
| `RQ=RQ22;LOCUS=cone-subspace;MECH=directions;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=across-generation;ARCHITECTURE=diffusion-language-model;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | Extract the refusal cone (not a single step-wise SRI direction) in a diffusion language model and track its geometry across denoising steps; P118/P128/P129 only show a per-step signal / trajectory attack, never a multi-dimensional cone. |

## §3-quater. Partial coverage: answered vs open (analysis notes)

For each point, our reading of what the literature already answers and what stays open.
The answered/open prose is human analysis kept for the reader (not part of the data
model). The `coverage` column IS part of the model: it is the cell's 2-level status —
`covered` (derived from the papers that sit there) or `required-uncovered` (an authored
empty gap from the `fieldmap-uncovered` block) — computed by the map alone, never from
edges. `demand` = future-work edges into the point, shown here only as context.

| Cell | Coverage | Demand | Answered | Still open |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `token__behavior` | covered | 5 | Token-level behavioral attacks are densely demonstrated and measured: GCG and its successors (P01, P03, P06, P10) reach near-100% ASR and set the refusal-failure baseline (RQ10/RQ13). | Why a suffix works mechanistically lives in the attention cells (token__attention/circuit-head__attention), not here; how durable an evasion stays under a model change is only partially tested (RQ5). |
| `direction__directions` | covered | 49 | That a causal refusal direction exists and steers refusal is thoroughly proven across 13 models / 7 families (P17, P19, P83), with eight catalogued overlap/ablation methods (section 9.3). | Whether one refusal direction transfers across families is disputed (P66/P67 show transfer vs. P69/P79 lab-specific), and the single-direction view is only partly reconciled with the multi-direction picture (P19: many geometric directions, one linear switch). |
| `cone-subspace__directions` | covered | 58 | Refusal/safety is shown to occupy a low-rank subspace rather than a 1-D line, with measured dimensionality and a task-agnostic over-refusal direction (P137, P152, P107); subspace rerouting steers within it (P29). | A rigorous count of independent cone directions for refusal, and transfer of refusal cones across families, stay open - cone transfer is shown only for truth (P84), not refusal (section 9.6). |
| `direction__training` | covered | 36 | The direction is shown to form early in pretraining (P126, P127) and to drift or relocate under instruction/harmful finetuning and compression (P109, P110, P116, P117). | How the NUMBER or cone of independent mechanisms changes along supervised-finetuning / rlhf-alignment / finetune-time / compress is not measured (RQ19). |
| `sparse-autoencoder-feature__sparse-autoencoders` | covered | 1 | sparse-autoencoders decomposition is established: harm and refusal features separate, jailbreaks suppress refusal features, and dormant 'hydra' backups survive single-feature ablation (P32, P33); features are similar across architectures (P35, P36, P94) (RQ9). | Linking feature redundancy to the depth of the suffix-vs-probe arms-race as a count-of-mechanisms meter is not done (RQ7). |
| `neuron__neurons` | covered | 0 | A small fraction of neurons (~1-5%, sometimes <0.3%) is shown to restore >90% of safety and to transfer cross-lingually and cross-architecture (P51, P52, P53, P54). | A rigorous estimate of the number of INDEPENDENT harm mechanisms (counting through the arms-race) is not done (RQ7). |
| `circuit-head__attention` | covered | 0 | Refusal and affirmation heads are identified and the adversarial suffix is shown to act as an attention-hijack that co-opts existing language circuits to suppress refusal (P25, P122). | Attention-hijack as a cross-model TRANSFER invariant is not studied (section 4 gap RQ7/RQ8 x attention). |
| `circuit-head__neurons` | covered | 0 | Trigger-conditioned defection is shown to be linearly detectable and to survive safety training (P121, 'Sleeper Agents'). | Beyond this single backdoor probe the neuron-level circuit of refusal - its count and transfer - is essentially untouched here (thin: one work). |
| `mixture-of-experts-expert__neurons` | covered | 0 | Safety-critical experts are identified in mixture-of-experts models and shown to be a positional vulnerability, with refusal partly a routing-to-safety-experts phenomenon (P80, P113, P114). | The number / cone of independent refusal mechanisms in mixture-of-experts is not measured (RQ22). |
| `token__attention` | covered | 0 | Universal suffixes are shown to be shallow attention-hijackers that capture chat-template tokens (P27). | Token-level attention coverage is thin (one work); whether the hijack is a transfer invariant is open (see section 3-bis). |
| `token__training` | covered | 1 | Safety alignment is shown to be 'shallow' - concentrated in the first output tokens - and the attack-depth vs alignment-depth arms-race is characterised (P30, P31) (RQ12). | Field future-work still asks to extend any-depth alignment under stronger attacks (edges). |
| `weights__directions` | covered | 0 | Permanent weight edits are demonstrated: rank-one abliteration bakes removal of the refusal direction into the weights (P17), and LEACE erases a linear concept in closed form (P57). | Only two methods, both single-direction and on dense text; transfer and multi-direction weight erasure are untested. |
| `RQ=RQ12;LOCUS=weights;MECH=training;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=finetune-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=across-training;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | required-uncovered | 11 | Nothing yet — 0 papers. | Study weight-level training as a refusal carrier: measure how training-time weight updates and curriculum or safety-neuron interventions (F13) move, erase or restore the refusal direction and whether that direction and geometry transfer across families and scales (F1). Test whether a geometry-preserving finetune defends it against finetuning-induced safety degradation (F11) and black-box transferable jailbreaks (F15), whether the weight-space analysis extends to non-refusal behaviors such as truth, sycophancy and persona (F6) and to mechanisms of harmful behavior beyond the refusal direction (F28), and standardize the layer-targeted steering used to read and restore it (F24). |
| `RQ=RQ22;LOCUS=mixture-of-experts-expert;MECH=directions;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=mixture-of-experts;IDENTIFIABILITY=yes` | required-uncovered | 4 | Nothing yet — 0 papers. | Extract refusal directions/cones per mixture-of-experts expert and test whether refusal is carried by routing vs within-expert directions, then transfer to a full-size mixture-of-experts. |
| `RQ=RQ7;LOCUS=neuron;MECH=training;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=partial;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=pretraining;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=across-training;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=yes` | required-uncovered | 1 | Nothing yet — 0 papers. | Track the emergence and stability of safety neurons across pretraining/finetuning checkpoints; does the universal-neuron share change with training and scale. |
| `RQ=RQ19;LOCUS=cone-subspace;MECH=training;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=finetune-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=across-training;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | required-uncovered | 3 | Nothing yet — 0 papers. | Measure how the refusal cone drifts, distorts or relocates across instruction/harmful finetuning, and whether the cone dimensionality itself changes. |
| `RQ=RQ6;LOCUS=circuit-head;MECH=directions;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=yes;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | required-uncovered | 3 | Nothing yet — 0 papers. | Integrate the representation and circuit views: determine whether a protective, jailbreak-resistant refusal circuit exists and what makes the removable latent circuit removable, testing whether that circuit and its geometry transfer across model families and scales (F1), whether it withstands black-box transferable jailbreaks (F15), and how it relates to mechanisms of harmful behavior beyond the refusal direction (F28). |
| `RQ=RQ2;LOCUS=cone-subspace;MECH=directions;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | required-uncovered | 0 | Nothing yet — 0 papers. | Extract the refusal cone optimally rather than as an existence-proof: current work only shows a refusal direction/cone exists with heuristic extraction, so derive the refusal subspace with a principled non-heuristic method, measure its true dimensionality, and test whether the cone (not just a single direction) transfers across models. |
| `RQ=RQ8;LOCUS=direction;MECH=directions;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=agent;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | required-uncovered | 0 | Nothing yet — 0 papers. | Test the refusal direction in the agentic setting: extract the inference-time refusal direction inside a tool-using agent loop and measure whether single-direction ablation still removes refusal across multi-step agent trajectories, and whether it transfers across agent backbones. |
| `RQ=RQ14;LOCUS=direction;MECH=directions;CODE=released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=emergent-misalignment;POLARITY=neutral;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | required-uncovered | 0 | Nothing yet — 0 papers. | Isolate an emergent-misalignment direction against refusal: extract a linear direction for emergent misalignment, test whether it is independent of or entangled with the refusal direction, and whether steering along it induces broad misalignment. |
| `RQ=RQ4;LOCUS=direction;MECH=training;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | required-uncovered | 4 | Nothing yet — 0 papers. | synthetic boundary-data \| AlphaEvolve auto-search \| robustness to a direct attack on the classifier not checked; probe integrated → may be worse \| intent linearly separable; specificity drops on OOD-benign → deploy calibration |
| `RQ=RQ6;LOCUS=direction;MECH=training;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | required-uncovered | 4 | Nothing yet — 0 papers. | Take the CSS+NAAT certificate beyond input-token (l0) robustness: test the defense across architectures and scales (F1), extend it to a latent-space certificate that holds against adaptive attacks (F20), and compare it to perturbation defenses such as char/word disruption, randomized selection and paraphrase smoothing (F21). |
| `RQ=RQ1;LOCUS=cone-subspace;MECH=directions;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | required-uncovered | 3 | Nothing yet — 0 papers. | Expand the direction registries and close the entangler-leak with a more reliable, automatic extraction of refusal directions and features (F5), and test whether within-model geodesic/nonlinear steering generalizes to a cross-model cone-to-cone map, i.e. whether refusal is erasable linearly or needs nonlinear methods (F2). |
| `RQ=RQ10;LOCUS=direction;MECH=directions;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | required-uncovered | 3 | Nothing yet — 0 papers. | Adversarial suffixes overfit to their source model (83% high-impact in-source but poor transfer): learn a masking that keeps the high-impact tokens while reducing source-distribution dependency, improving the suffix and prompt optimizer (F14) and the resulting black-box, cross-model transferability (F15). |
| `RQ=RQ15;LOCUS=direction;MECH=training;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | required-uncovered | 3 | Nothing yet — 0 papers. | ACT recovers a refusal direction robust to adaptive attacks and CoT replacement, but the suffix-versus-probe race under reasoning regeneration was never run: study reasoning-model (LRM) safety directly (F31), testing whether prompt-level CoT-hijacks like H-CoT and activation-level evasion transfer to reasoning models and whether ACT's direction survives as a monitor lever. |
| `RQ=RQ14;LOCUS=cone-subspace;MECH=directions;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=sycophancy;POLARITY=neutral;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | required-uncovered | 3 | Nothing yet — 0 papers. | causal separation of sycophancy is linear (3 independent directions); nonlinear/RepInd count and a cone for arbitrary behavior are open \| sycophancy in attention-heads, partially overlaps truthful, independently-modulable; the nonlinear structure not exhausted (linear>nonlinear probe) \| complements the readout geometry P137 (low-rank subspaces of non-refusal) and persona P88; paired with P154/P155 (sycophancy cluster); nonlinear/RepInd count open |
| `RQ=RQ1;LOCUS=cone-subspace;MECH=directions;CODE=released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | required-uncovered | 3 | Nothing yet — 0 papers. | Establish that the refusal direction is causal rather than correlational (F4) with a standardized, layer-targeted ablation (F24), then test whether multi-shot and multi-turn role-play attacks (F17) bypass it through a different mechanism than the single-turn Arditi/Rimsky conditioning. |
| `RQ=RQ4;LOCUS=cone-subspace;MECH=directions;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | required-uncovered | 2 | Nothing yet — 0 papers. | INNSTEER yields an invertible nonlinear map within a single model, but the cross-model cone-to-cone correspondence is open: test whether refusal is erasable linearly or whether such a nonlinear map is required (F2) by transporting the cone across models and measuring whether linear ablation alone suffices. |
| `RQ=RQ1;LOCUS=cone-subspace;MECH=directions;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=harmfulness;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | required-uncovered | 2 | Nothing yet — 0 papers. | Move past the existence-proof: map the layer-wise circuit linking the harmfulness representation (t_inst) to the refusal representation (t_post), counting the mechanisms of harmful behavior that lie beyond the single refusal direction (F28) and quantifying the geometric coupling between the under- and over-refusal cones so that mitigating over-refusal (F7) does not silently re-introduce harmful compliance. |
| `RQ=RQ1;LOCUS=cone-subspace;MECH=directions;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=multi;POLARITY=neutral;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | required-uncovered | 2 | Nothing yet — 0 papers. | Pin down the read-versus-steer gap where the refusal signal reads and transfers but steering fails to move behavior (F34): run ablations crossed with paraphrase, scale and temperature under a rigorously powered evaluation pipeline with a justified sample size (F25), separating a genuine causal steering failure from underpowered or confounded measurement. |
| `RQ=RQ5;LOCUS=direction;MECH=training;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | required-uncovered | 2 | Nothing yet — 0 papers. | Turn the currently empirical, white-box layer-susceptibility result into a standardized, layer-targeted activation-steering method (F24) that bypasses the most susceptible layers without fine-tuning, and test it for certified or provable robustness (F20) against the adaptive and obfuscation attacks whose resistance is so far unproven. |
| `RQ=RQ9;LOCUS=cone-subspace;MECH=directions;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | required-uncovered | 2 | Nothing yet — 0 papers. | Use SAE-based feature analysis of refusal (F27) to weigh the risks and benefits of redundant refusal features, and measure how role-play and multi-turn attacks (F17) perturb those features' activations across a conversation, distinguishing robust redundancy from exploitable redundancy. |
| `RQ=RQ21;LOCUS=cone-subspace;MECH=directions;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=across-generation;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | required-uncovered | 2 | Nothing yet — 0 papers. | ROI transfer to 70B+; encoded/Base64 inputs \| alignment with the refusal direction does not predict robustness |
| `RQ=RQ10;LOCUS=cone-subspace;MECH=directions;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=black-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | required-uncovered | 2 | Nothing yet — 0 papers. | Safety-Net measures scalar vulnerability to jailbreak (best-of-N), NOT the number of independent mechanisms → open \| Safety-Net (margin, Thm2) — a scalar measure; adjacent to the measures P133/P150, but NOT a count of independent mechanisms (A) |
| `RQ=RQ10;LOCUS=direction;MECH=directions;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=yes;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | required-uncovered | 2 | Nothing yet — 0 papers. | Build on the exponentiated-gradient relaxation to craft readable adversarial prompts that transfer better than gibberish suffixes: improve the underlying suffix and prompt optimizer (F14) and measure black-box transfer of the readable attacks across models (F15). |

| `RQ=RQ18;LOCUS=direction;MECH=directions;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=no;MODALITY=vision-language;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | required-uncovered | 2 | Nothing yet — 0 papers. | VLM refusal encoding looks ~50-PC and ablation-robust, but that is PCA variation plus a single behavioral dimension, not a strict representational-independence or causal count: measure the true dimensionality of the refusal cone outside text and the attacks and defenses on it for vision-language models (F8), where cross-model evasion currently transfers at 11% or less. |
| `RQ=RQ1;LOCUS=cone-subspace;MECH=directions;CODE=released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=partial;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | required-uncovered | 2 | Nothing yet — 0 papers. | Current extraction is a heuristic existence-proof shown on one model and one example: make it optimal and automatic (F5), for instance via gradient- or SSR-based discovery, and test whether the extracted direction and its geometry transfer across model families and scales (F1) rather than holding only for the demonstrated case. |
| `RQ=RQ13;LOCUS=direction;MECH=training;CODE=released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=black-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | required-uncovered | 2 | Nothing yet — 0 papers. | The threat model is truncated (no system-prompt or prefill) and text-only: extend it to multimodal attacks and defenses (F8) and train against a diverse attack set so adversarial training yields generalizable robustness (F23) rather than overfitting to one attack family like R2D2. |
| `RQ=RQ10;LOCUS=direction;MECH=training;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=black-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | required-uncovered | 2 | Nothing yet — 0 papers. | Static heuristics break on frontier models, a generalization gap that only widens with scale where fine-tuning merely delays the failure and adaptive variants reappear: measure robustness across attacker and defender budgets and model scale (F18) and pair it with probe-based monitoring whose OOD generalization is tested (F19). |
| `RQ=RQ6;LOCUS=direction;MECH=training;CODE=released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | required-uncovered | 2 | Nothing yet — 0 papers. | Turn the empirical denoising and paraphrase smoothing defense into a certified one: prove a lower-bound accuracy and a certificate on the probability filter (F20), and benchmark it against the family of inference-time perturbation defenses such as smoothing, paraphrase and randomization (F21), accounting for the computational burden it imposes on the attacker. |
| `RQ=RQ24;LOCUS=cone-subspace;MECH=directions;CODE=released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=yes` | required-uncovered | 2 | Nothing yet — 0 papers. | diverse targets / RL-judge reward → find MORE independent mechanisms \| do not rule out yet-undiscovered regions mediating refusal |
| `RQ=RQ1;LOCUS=direction;MECH=training;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | required-uncovered | 1 | Nothing yet — 0 papers. | No study evaluates inference-time perturbation defenses against a training-derived refusal direction: test whether perplexity filtering, paraphrasing and self-reminder prompts degrade or preserve the white-box refusal signal, and whether smoothing and randomization defenses (F21) blunt direction-based jailbreaks without harming benign refusal. |
| `RQ=RQ4;LOCUS=cone-subspace;MECH=directions;CODE=released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=no;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | required-uncovered | 1 | Nothing yet — 0 papers. | Show that linear-probe accuracy is non-diagnostic of the refusal cone: build a rigorous probe and jailbreak evaluation pipeline (F25) with held-out harmful categories and calibrated baselines, so a high-accuracy probe is not mistaken for a faithful read of the refusal subspace. |

| `RQ=RQ22;LOCUS=cone-subspace;MECH=directions;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=mixture-of-experts;IDENTIFIABILITY=yes` | required-uncovered | 1 | Nothing yet — 0 papers. | Extract a per-expert refusal cone in a mixture-of-experts model and test whether refusal dimensionality is concentrated in 'safety experts' (P80/P113) or distributed; existing MoE work (P114) studies routing vs within-expert at the neuron level, not the cone. |
| `RQ=RQ18;LOCUS=cone-subspace;MECH=directions;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=audio;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | required-uncovered | 1 | Nothing yet — 0 papers. | Extract a multi-DIMENSIONAL refusal cone (not a single direction) inside a large audio-language model and measure its dimensionality, as P152 did for vision-language; current audio work (P135/P136) only recovers a single 'golden' direction. |
| `RQ=RQ24;LOCUS=cone-subspace;MECH=directions;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=truth;POLARITY=neutral;DYNAMICS=static;ARCHITECTURE=dense;IDENTIFIABILITY=yes` | required-uncovered | 1 | Nothing yet — 0 papers. | Apply the refusal counting/non-uniqueness apparatus (RQ24) to a non-refusal behavior: extract the truth subspace, measure its dimensionality and whether many directions give the same behavioral shift, paralleling P84's truth cones. |
| `RQ=RQ22;LOCUS=cone-subspace;MECH=directions;CODE=not-released;MODELS=multi;TRANSFER=not-tested;MODALITY=text;ACCESS=white-box;STAGE=inference-time;BEHAVIOR=refusal;POLARITY=under-refusal;DYNAMICS=across-generation;ARCHITECTURE=diffusion-language-model;IDENTIFIABILITY=no` | required-uncovered | 1 | Nothing yet — 0 papers. | Extract the refusal cone (not a single step-wise SRI direction) in a diffusion language model and track its geometry across denoising steps; P118/P128/P129 only show a per-step signal / trajectory attack, never a multi-dimensional cone. |

> All 88 `required-uncovered` points enumerated above by their full coordinate (the 8 authored core gaps first, then the 76 materialized future-work targets, then the 4 greenfield-anchored gaps) hold zero papers. They ARE materialized cells in the map — authored in the `fieldmap-uncovered` block as FULL tensor points (a value on every axis, the spec of the covering paper) and a status of the cell — and the site paints them red on the coverage map (Icicle/Sunburst "covered only" and "full grid", and the Pivot Heatmap grid). This is wholly independent of the edges: an edge target that lands on one of these authored gaps reads its coverage from the map, never the reverse.

---

## 4. Reading lenses (optional)

The all-axes coverage rollup in §3 is the canonical coverage view. The tables below are
optional reading lenses over particular axes — each is one lens among equals, and no axis
or pair is privileged.

### 4-a. Lens: RQ × MECH (which lens looked where)

Cell notation: `D`=strong works exist, `p`=partial, `.`=empty/weak.

| RQ \ MECH | behavior | directions | sparse-autoencoders | neurons | attention | training |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| RQ2 geometry/count of directions | . | **D** P18/P19 | p P19 | p P51/P52 | . | . |
| RQ5 monitor evasion | D P38 | D P38 | D P38 | p P37 | p P27 | D P38 |
| RQ6 defense | D P47/P49 | p P41 | . | p P51 | . | D P39/P40 |
| RQ7 count of schemas | . | p P56 | p P33 | **p** P51/P52 | . | . |
| RQ8 transfer | D P01/P13 | p P22 | p P35/P36 | p P53 | . | . |

**Empty/weak cells (dig candidates):**
1. **RQ7 × directions/sparse-autoencoders via arms-race** — **NOT COVERED:** almost empty. No one estimated the number of *independent* harm mechanisms as the depth of the suffix↔probe race.
2. **RQ8 × directions (refusal cones) and × behavior (probe evasion)** — **NOT COVERED:** transfer of **refusal cones** and of evasion across families has not been done (P22 transfers single SVs; P84 transferred cones only for **truth**, not refusal).
3. **RQ7/RQ8 × attention** — **NOT COVERED:** attention-hijack as a transfer invariant is not studied.

### 4-bis. Slice along the NEW axes (where the tensor was "collapsed") — revision 8

Almost the entire catalog implicitly sat at a single point of each new axis. These are exactly the large empty branches:

| New axis | Dominant value (where everything is) | Filled at the edges | Empty cells (dig) |
|---|---|---|---|
| **MODALITY** | `text` | vision-language: P101/P102 (collapse, shared neurons) | NOT COVERED: refusal **cones/RepInd/count/probe evasion** in vision-language/audio/video — almost empty |
| **ACCESS** | `white-box` | grey-box: P103 (probe-as-attack); black-box: P09/P12 | NOT COVERED: **co-design of attack↔probe (grey-box) as a meter of the number of mechanisms** — grey-box arms-race |
| **STAGE** | `inference-time` | finetune-time: P109; compress: P110; drift: ProCon | NOT COVERED: **evolution of the number/cone of mechanisms along supervised-finetuning → rlhf-alignment → finetune-time → compress** |
| **LOCUS** | `direction` | feature P32/P94, neuron P51/P95, expert P80, weights P53 | NOT COVERED: **a unified ladder "how many independent units" across all levels at once** |
| **BEHAVIOR** | `refusal` | truth P72/P84, traits P88, emergent-misalignment P73 | NOT COVERED: **cones/RepInd/count/arms-race for NON-refusal** (RQ14) |
| **POLARITY** | `under-refusal` | over-refusal: P107/P108 | NOT COVERED: **cones/RepInd/count/transfer for over-refusal** (RQ17) |

**Main structural conclusion:** the map was a 3D slice (`RQ × MECH × refusal/text/white-box/inference-time`). These 6 axes turn it into an honest tensor; most of the "new" cells are **empty or barely touched** — especially `count of independent mechanisms (RQ7)` and `cone transfer (RQ8)`, multiplied across MODALITY/STAGE/BEHAVIOR/POLARITY. "Count of independent mechanisms" is not a single gap but a **whole row of gaps** along the new axes.

---

## 5. Cross-model transfer (optional reading) — RQ8 in detail

| What is transferred | Status | Work |
|---|---|---|
| Adversarial suffixes across models | PARTIAL (often fails; LoRA breaks it) | P01, P13, P28, 2602.06630 |
| A single steering vector (concept) | DONE (the linear map works) | P22 |
| **Refusal DIRECTION across families** | **CONTESTED**: transfer P66 (recipe in concept-basis), P67 (cross-lingual) · refute P69+P79 (lab-specific, cross-model fails) | P66, P67, P69, P79 |
| sparse-autoencoders features across architectures | PARTIAL | P35, P36 |
| Safety neurons across models/languages | PARTIAL (cross-arch ASR 78.5%) | P53, P54 |
| Deception/sycophancy directions (NOT refusal) | PARTIAL (zero-shot on new datasets) | P76 |
| Cones (multidimensional) across architectures — for **truth** | **PARTIAL→DONE for truth** | P84 (cones generalize across arch.) |
| **Refusal CONES specifically across models (RepInd)** | **OPEN** | — (P84 did it for truth, not refusal; P66 transfers only 1 direction) |
| **Probe evasion** | **OPEN** | — |

→ RQ8 is a disputed axis: the single refusal direction transfers (P66/P67) or not (P69/P79) — not reconciled. **Cone transfer has already been shown for truth (P84)** → for refusal it remains open, but as a "delta" rather than a blank spot. Unclaimed: **transfer of refusal cones**, **transfer of probe evasion**, **dependence of transfer on size/architecture**.

---

## 6. Model coverage (optional reading) — MODELS axis

- Vicuna-7B: P01, P03, P25 — saturated.
- Gemma-2-2b / Llama-3-8B: P18, P32, P33, P38 — saturated (sparse-autoencoders exists: Gemma Scope/Llama Scope).
- Llama-3.2-1B: P19 — cheap for iterative experiments.
- Qwen-2.5: P06, P04 — attacks.
- 72B: only P17 (single direction).
- **Cross-family comparative analysis of refusal geometry — almost none** (see §5).

---

## 8. Main tensor takeaways
1. Closed: existence and multiplicity of refusal directions (P17/P18/P19/P82), the suffix mechanism (P17/P26/P27), evasion of linear/sparse-autoencoders probes (P38), sparse-autoencoders core+tail and hydra (P33).
2. Gaps (after revision 24): **RQ7** (causal count of *independent* mechanisms — the race as a **meter**; cone dim already P18/P19/P82), **RQ15** (refusal in reasoning/chain-of-thought — almost empty, P99/P100), **RQ8-remainder** (transfer of **refusal cones** + transfer of **probe evasion** + transfer of the **NUMBER** of mechanisms), **RQ16** (necessity test for the refusal probe), **RQ6** (adaptive defense — P85/P86), **RQ14** (non-refusal directions — taken by P88/P115/P137/P140; what remains is causal count/transfer of the number).
3. Transfer of a *single* refusal direction — a **dispute** P66/P67 ↔ P69/P79 (transfers / lab-specific), unresolved.
4. **Shifts in the literature:** (a) "arms-race" as a frame is **already published** (P92 ACE-Safety, P93 Metis) → what's open is only **counting the number of independent mechanisms through the race**, not the race itself; (b) **predict-control is filling up** (P96–P98), but a rigorous necessity/silence test specifically for the refusal probe is not yet done; (c) a **fresh reasoning-refusal axis** appeared (P99/P100), the least covered; (d) cone transfer for truth (P84) + crosscoders/univ.neurons (P94/P95) filled the transfer toolbox.
5. **Structural expansion:** the tensor was "collapsed" along 6 axes (MODALITY/ACCESS/STAGE/LOCUS/BEHAVIOR/POLARITY); along them almost everything is empty (§4-bis). New confirmed branches: the **over-refusal** geometry is separate and higher-dimensional (P107); safety geometry **collapses in VLMs** (P101); the refusal direction **drifts under finetune** and **shifts under pruning, but not under quantization** (P109/P110/ProCon); **the probe can be used as an attack** (grey-box, P103).

---

## 9. Refusal direction: deep dive

> Focus on the question "how well confirmed is the refusal direction, does it transfer, how are they
> overlapped, is it proven that there are many of them". All 5 slices below.

### 9.1 Three anchor works: how well proven

| Work | What it proves | Method | Models | Code | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **P17 Arditi** (2406.11717, NeurIPS24) | refusal is governed by a **1-dimensional** subspace: erase → does not refuse; add → refuses on a harmless one | difference-of-means (harmful/benign contrast), best-layer selection | **13 models, 7 families up to 72B** (Qwen 1.8–72B, Yi 6/34B, Gemma 2/7B, Llama-2 7/13/70B, Llama-3 8/70B) | + | DONE, thorough (capability preserved: MMLU/ARC/GSM8K/TruthfulQA ≈ unchanged) |
| **P18 Wollschläger** (2502.17420, ICML25) | the direction is **NOT one**: a set of independent directions + **multidimensional cones (up to dim 5)**; larger model → higher cone dimensionality | gradient RDO (loss = ablation CE + addition + retain-KL) + RepInd constraint | Gemma-2 (2B/9B), Qwen2.5 (1.5/3/7/14B), Llama-3 8B | + | DONE |
| **P19 More to Refusal** (2602.02132, 2026) | 11 categories = geometrically different directions (cos 0.4–0.6, some nearly orthogonal), **BUT linear steering = one common "switch"**; the difference is not "whether it refuses" but "how it refuses" | mean-diff over 11 splits + sparse-autoencoders | Gemma-2-9b, Llama-3.x | ± (HF data) | DONE, recent |

**Comment:** "one direction" (Arditi) and "many directions" (Wollschläger) are **reconciled** in P19 — geometrically there are many directions, but linear control collapses into one degree of freedom (a 1D switch for "whether it refuses"). The topic is very well covered.

### 9.2 Transferability to other models

| What is transferred | Proven? | Detail / work |
|---|---|---|
| The refusal direction itself (one vector across models) | **no — each has its own** | Arditi extracts the direction **separately for each model** |
| The effect itself (every model has such a direction) | **DONE** | 13 models, 7 families (P17) |
| Transfer of an arbitrary steering vector via a linear map | **DONE** | P22 (Platonic, ACL25): a linear map + weak-to-strong |
| Transfer of safety neurons across models/languages | **PARTIAL** | P53 (<0.3% of neurons, cross-lingual), P54 (cross-arch ASR 78.5%) |
| Transfer of CONES across architectures (truth) | **DONE for truth** | P84 (cones generalize) — but NOT refusal |
| **Transfer of refusal CONES specifically** | **OPEN** | no one did it for refusal ← a gap (but the method exists, P84) |
| **Transfer of probe evasion** | **OPEN** | — ← a gap |

**Comment:** "the effect is universal, the geometry is model-specific". Cone transfer as a **method** is already shown (P84, truth) — for **refusal** specifically it was not done; this + transfer of probe evasion are open gaps.

### 9.3 Methods of "overlapping" (ablation) directions

| Method | Work | Code | What it does | Granularity / permanence |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Directional ablation (project out, all layers/positions) | P17 | + | removes the r component from activations | inference-time, 1 direction |
| Activation addition (+αr) | P17, P19 | + | induces refusal | inference-time |
| **Weight orthogonalization / rank-one edit (abliteration)** | P17 | + | bakes the removal of r into the weights | permanent, 1 direction |
| RDO (gradient search of directions/cones) | P18 | + | optimizes for ablation+retain | builds a cone |
| **RepInd constraint** | P18 | + | finds a direction whose ablation does not affect another | yields independent directions |
| **Iterative ablation with recomputation (K directions)** | P19 / HF `iterated-refusal-ablation` | ± | erase → recompute → find the next | a direct test of "are there any left" |
| sparse-autoencoders feature ablation (zero the latents) | P32, P33, P19 | + | removes refusal features | by features |
| **LEACE concept erasure** (closed-form) | P57 | + | removes all linearly-available info about the concept at once | a whole linear subspace |

### 9.4 "There are many directions and after overlapping some remain" — evidence

| Sub-claim | Proven? | Direct evidence |
|---|---|---|
| There are several directions (geometrically) | **DONE** | P18 (cones dim≤5), P19 (cos 0.4–0.6) |
| They are functionally independent (RepInd) | **DONE** | P18: ablation of one does not affect another |
| **After ablating one — refusal remains (backup paths)** | **DONE** | P19: Llama does not react to ablation of the safety direction → "richer structure with **redundant paths that survived ablation**" |
| Dormant backup mechanisms turn on when the first ones are suppressed | **DONE** | P33 "hydra" features: dormant until you suppress the first ones |
| Iterative overlapping of K directions | **DONE/PARTIAL** | HF iterated-ablation: K directions with recomputation |

### 9.5 Correlation with other circuit works (different lenses on the same thing)

| Lens | What it says about refusal | Consistency with directions |
|---|---|---|
| `directions` directions | a 1D switch + cones | baseline |
| `sparse-autoencoders` features | core (~500 latents/layer: 591/517/421 at L9/20/31) + a long tail of style/domain | **high**: sparse-autoencoders directions cos 0.85–0.97 with activation-space (P19) |
| `neurons` neurons | ~1–5% of safety neurons restore >90% of safety (P51/P52); cross-lingual transfer (P53) | medium; alignment-tax overlap |
| `attention` attention | refusal/affirmation heads; suffix = attention-hijack (P25, P27) | medium |
| Universality of features across models | sparse-autoencoders features similar Transf↔Mamba (P35), USAE (P36), Platonic (P23); **truth cones generalize (P84)**; jailbreaks transfer by representational proximity (P81) | supports transfer (for refusal **cones** not checked ← a gap) |

### 9.6 Transfer of cones/directions: a linear or nonlinear dependence?

| What we transfer | Dependence type (proven) | Work | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| A single SV (any concept) across models | **a linear/affine map** (learned) | P22 | DONE |
| A refusal direction across languages (1 model) | **parallelism** of vectors ≈ linear/identity | P67 | DONE |
| A refusal direction across families | **locally-linear** reconstruction in a shared concept-basis (ridge) **+ nonlinear layer alignment (DTW)** + a linear SVD-guard | P66 | PARTIAL (disputed vs P69) |
| Independence of directions WITHIN a model | RepInd catches **both linear and nonlinear** dependencies (cosine across all layers) | P18 | DONE |
| **Transfer of a CONE (multidimensional) across models — truth** | **shown** (cones generalize across arch.), the mapping type not formally characterized | P84 | PARTIAL |
| **Transfer of a refusal CONE across models** | **unknown** — a linear map (P22)? piecewise-linear? nonlinear? | — | **OPEN** (the method exists, P84) |
| **Dependence of transfer quality on Δsize/architecture** | P66 gives only diagnostics (spectral agreement, geometric distortion), no formal dependence | P66 | **OPEN** |

**Conclusion on the question (updated, revision 6):** transfer of *directions* is described as **locally-linear in a shared semantic basis** (P22/P66/P67); the "nonlinearity" of P66 is the DTW layer alignment, not the vector geometry. Transfer of **cones as a method is already shown for truth** (P84: "cones generalize across architectures"), but (a) **not for refusal** and (b) **the mapping type is not formally characterized** (a linear map? piecewise-linear by facets? dependent on Δdim/Δscale?). The precise open formulation: "do **refusal cones** specifically transfer (P84 did it only for truth), what is the (non)linearity of the cone→cone mapping, and is it consistent with the P66↔P69 dispute".

**Deep-dive bottom line:** the theory is very well confirmed (13 models); "there are many directions, independent, and after overlapping some remain" is proven (P18 + P19 + P33 + P82 SOM); there are 8 overlapping methods, almost all with code. Transfer of a *single* direction across families is **disputedly resolved** (P66/P67 ↔ P69/P79). Transfer of **cones** as a method is **already shown for truth (P84)**, but for **refusal** specifically not. Unclosed: **transfer of refusal CONES + its linearity** (§9.6) and **transfer of probe evasion**.

---
